title: M006-E004-S001: Extend measurement spec types for entity definitions
description:
## Story: M006-E004-S001

Add EntityDefinition and EntityProperty interfaces to types.ts. Extend MeasurementSpec with optional entities field and TrackingEvent with optional entity_id. Backwards compatible.

### Acceptance Criteria
- EntityProperty: name, type (string|number|boolean|array), description, isRequired
- EntityDefinition: id, name, description, properties[]
- MeasurementSpec includes optional entities: EntityDefinition[]
- TrackingEvent includes optional entity_id: string
- Types align with measurementPlan.ts schema shape
- Existing specs without entities still type-check
- Existing TrackingEvent without entity_id still type-checks

### Key Files
- convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts
- convex/schema.ts
- convex/measurementPlan.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Fix both pre-existing type/runtime mismatches (EventProperty.isRequired, coverage sub-object) alongside new entity types — types catching up to reality
- Keep EntityProperty and EventProperty as separate interfaces (different domain concepts: entity schema vs event payload)
- EntityDefinition.id is LLM-generated string identifier, not a Convex DB reference
- Use strict type union for EntityProperty.type matching existing EventProperty pattern

### Simplification Review
- Reviewer questioned EntityDefinition on MeasurementSpec (separation of concerns) — rejected per AC requirements
- Reviewer suggested merging EntityProperty/EventProperty — rejected per AC, different domain concepts
- Valid point: three type systems need alignment — this story fixes two existing drift points
- No unnecessary abstractions — just interfaces and optional fields

### Final Design
Add EntityProperty and EntityDefinition interfaces to types.ts. Add isRequired to EventProperty. Restructure MeasurementSpec coverage to sub-object. Add optional entities and entity_id fields. Update parser to use isRequired. Update tests.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-12-measurement-entity-types-design.md

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: (1) types.ts changes, (2) parser fix in generateMeasurementSpec.ts (required→isRequired), (3) test updates for both existing and new types

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

---

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Extend the measurement spec output type system with EntityDefinition and EntityProperty interfaces, fix two existing type-vs-runtime mismatches (EventProperty missing `isRequired`, MeasurementSpec flat coverage fields vs nested `coverage` sub-object), and add optional `entities` and `entity_id` fields to MeasurementSpec and TrackingEvent. All changes are backwards compatible — existing specs and events without entity data continue to type-check.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts` exports `EntityProperty` interface with fields: `name: string`, `type: "string" | "number" | "boolean" | "array"`, `description: string`, `isRequired: boolean`
- [ ] `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts` exports `EntityDefinition` interface with fields: `id: string`, `name: string`, `description: string`, `properties: EntityProperty[]`
- [ ] `MeasurementSpec` in `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts` has field `entities?: EntityDefinition[]` (optional)
- [ ] `TrackingEvent` in `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts` has field `entity_id?: string` (optional)
- [ ] `EventProperty` in `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts` has field `isRequired: boolean` added (fixing pre-existing type drift)
- [ ] `MeasurementSpec` in `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts` has `coverage: { activation_levels_covered: number[]; value_moments_covered: string[] }` instead of flat fields (fixing pre-existing type drift)
- [ ] Parser in `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.ts` line 269 uses `isRequired: prop.required === true` instead of `required: prop.required === true`
- [ ] `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.test.ts` exits with code 0 (all existing tests pass with updated types)
- [ ] Test file `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.test.ts` includes test for EventProperty `isRequired` field on parsed properties
- [ ] Test file `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.test.ts` includes test verifying MeasurementSpec with optional `entities` field (present and absent)
- [ ] Test file `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.test.ts` includes test verifying TrackingEvent with optional `entity_id` field (present and absent)

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Add EntityProperty and EntityDefinition interfaces to types.ts**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts`
   - Change: Add `EntityProperty` interface (after `EventProperty`, around line 67) with fields: `name: string`, `type: "string" | "number" | "boolean" | "array"`, `description: string`, `isRequired: boolean`
   - Change: Add `EntityDefinition` interface (after `EntityProperty`) with fields: `id: string`, `name: string`, `description: string`, `properties: EntityProperty[]`

2. **Add isRequired to EventProperty**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts`
   - Change: Add `isRequired: boolean` field to `EventProperty` interface (line 63-66)

3. **Add optional entity_id to TrackingEvent**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts`
   - Change: Add `entity_id?: string` field to `TrackingEvent` interface (line 73-80)

4. **Restructure MeasurementSpec coverage to sub-object and add optional entities**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts`
   - Change: Replace flat fields `activation_levels_covered: number[]` and `value_moments_covered: string[]` with `coverage: { activation_levels_covered: number[]; value_moments_covered: string[] }` on `MeasurementSpec` (line 82-89)
   - Change: Add `entities?: EntityDefinition[]` field to `MeasurementSpec`

5. **Fix parser EventProperty field name: required → isRequired**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.ts`
   - Change: Line 269 — change `required: prop.required === true` to `isRequired: prop.required === true`

6. **Add tests for isRequired, entities, entity_id**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.test.ts`
   - Change: Add test asserting parsed EventProperty has `isRequired` field (not `required`)
   - Change: Add test that a MeasurementSpec without `entities` field type-checks and parses
   - Change: Add test that a TrackingEvent without `entity_id` type-checks and parses
   - Change: Add test verifying `makeValidEvent` with `entity_id` parses correctly (entity_id passed through on raw event)

### Test Plan
- [ ] Run `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.test.ts` — all existing tests pass (they already use `spec.coverage.activation_levels_covered` shape)
- [ ] Verify new test for `isRequired`: parse a response, check `spec.events[0].properties[0].isRequired === true`
- [ ] Verify new test for entities absent: parse a response without entities, check `spec.entities === undefined`
- [ ] Verify new test for entity_id absent: parse a response, check `spec.events[0].entity_id === undefined`
- [ ] Run full `npm run test:run` — no regressions in other test files

title: M008-E003-S002: Implement website crawler for marketing pages
description:
## Mission: M008 Open Source Foundation
## Epic: E003 Modular Crawlers

### Outcome
WebsiteCrawler crawls marketing site — homepage, features, about, help/docs. Classifies page types, extracts clean text from HTML.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Implements Crawler interface
- canCrawl() true for HTTP/HTTPS URLs
- Discovers linked pages (features, about, pricing, docs)
- Classifies pages by URL patterns
- Extracts clean text (strips nav, footer, scripts)
- Respects CrawlOptions
- Tests use HTML fixtures

### Implementation Hints
Current codebase uses @mendable/firecrawl-js. Options: keep Firecrawl, use cheerio+fetch, or make pluggable. Page classification by URL pattern (/pricing, /features, /about, /docs).

title: Apply refinements to extraction based on validation findings
description:
## Context

**Mission M002:** Discover and model multi-level activation from product content
**Epic M002-E004:** Activation Level Validation & Refinement

**Story M002-E004-S004:** Apply refinements to extraction based on validation findings

**Dependencies:** Requires S003 (validation assessment) to be complete.

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** `docs/plans/2026-02-05-apply-refinements-impl.md`

### Overview
Build the activation level extractor following established patterns, create an accuracy test script, then iterate on refinements until 70%+ accuracy is achieved against sample products (Miro, Linear, Figma).

### Tasks
10 TDD tasks covering:
- Task 1: Create ActivationLevel types and interfaces
- Task 2: Add ACTIVATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT constant
- Task 3: Add page filtering logic
- Task 4: Add response parsing and validation
- Task 5: Create extractActivationLevels internalAction
- Task 6: Create accuracy test script
- Task 7: Run baseline accuracy test
- Task 8: Apply refinements based on baseline results
- Task 9: Iterate until 70% accuracy
- Task 10: Final verification and cleanup

### Testing
- `npm test -- extractActivationLevels` - Unit/integration tests
- `node scripts/test-activation-accuracy.mjs` - Accuracy validation

### Acceptance Criteria
1. [manual] Specific refinements are documented based on validation failures
2. [unit] ACTIVATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT is implemented and tested
3. [unit] filterActivationPages is implemented and tested
4. [integration] extractActivationLevels stores results in definitions.activation
5. [manual] Post-refinement validation achieves 70%+ accuracy target
6. [manual] Refinements and rationale documented in git commit history

title: M012-E001-S002: Entity and Perspective Types
description:
Mission: M012 - Double Three-Layer Measurement Spec
Epic: E001 - Core Types and Zod Schemas
Story: S002 - Entity and Perspective Types

## What
Define entity types and perspective containers:
- ProductEntity (id, name, description, isHeartbeat, properties: EntityProperty[], activities: ProductActivity[])
- CustomerEntity (name, properties: EntityProperty[], activities: CustomerActivity[])
- InteractionEntity (name, properties: EntityProperty[], activities: InteractionActivity[])
- EntityJsonSchema (entityName, perspective, schema: Record<string, unknown>)

## Acceptance Criteria
- ProductEntity has id (string), name, description, isHeartbeat (boolean), properties (EntityProperty[]), activities (ProductActivity[])
- CustomerEntity has name, properties (EntityProperty[]), activities (CustomerActivity[])
- InteractionEntity has name, properties (EntityProperty[]), activities (InteractionActivity[])
- EntityJsonSchema has entityName (string), perspective (Perspective), schema (Record<string, unknown>)
- All Zod schemas validate entities with nested activities and properties
- Type tests and schema tests exist for each entity type

## Files to Modify
- packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts
- packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts
- packages/core/src/types/__tests__/outputs.test.ts
- packages/core/src/schema/__tests__/outputs.test.ts

## Context
Activities nest inside entities — key structural difference from old design.
ProductEntity replaces old EntityDefinition but adds activities.
The id field stays (lowercase, matching /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/).
EntityJsonSchema.schema is Record<string, unknown> — actual generation logic is in E003.

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Add four new types and corresponding Zod schemas for entity containers in the Double Three-Layer Framework. ProductEntity, CustomerEntity, and InteractionEntity each embed their respective activity types (from S001) alongside EntityProperty arrays. EntityJsonSchema is a container for generated JSON Schema output, linking an entity name to a perspective and an arbitrary schema object. All types are added alongside existing ones (old types removed in S003).

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts` exports `ProductEntity` interface with fields: `id: string`, `name: string`, `description: string`, `isHeartbeat: boolean`, `properties: EntityProperty[]`, `activities: ProductActivity[]`
- [ ] `packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts` exports `CustomerEntity` interface with fields: `name: string`, `properties: EntityProperty[]`, `activities: CustomerActivity[]`
- [ ] `packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts` exports `InteractionEntity` interface with fields: `name: string`, `properties: EntityProperty[]`, `activities: InteractionActivity[]`
- [ ] `packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts` exports `EntityJsonSchema` interface with fields: `entityName: string`, `perspective: Perspective`, `schema: Record<string, unknown>`
- [ ] `packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts` exports `ProductEntitySchema` Zod schema that validates ProductEntity objects with nested EntityProperty and ProductActivity arrays
- [ ] `packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts` exports `CustomerEntitySchema` Zod schema that validates CustomerEntity objects with nested EntityProperty and CustomerActivity arrays
- [ ] `packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts` exports `InteractionEntitySchema` Zod schema that validates InteractionEntity objects with nested EntityProperty and InteractionActivity arrays
- [ ] `packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts` exports `EntityJsonSchemaSchema` Zod schema that validates EntityJsonSchema objects using PerspectiveSchema for perspective field
- [ ] `packages/core/src/types/__tests__/outputs.test.ts` has type-level tests for each of the 4 new entity/schema types
- [ ] `packages/core/src/schema/__tests__/outputs.test.ts` has Zod validation tests (accept valid, reject invalid) for each of the 4 new schemas
- [ ] `npm test` in `packages/core` exits with code 0

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Add entity type interfaces to types file**
   - File: `packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts`
   - Change: After the existing `EntityDefinition` interface (~line 83), add a new section "Double Three-Layer Entity Types" with:
     - `ProductEntity` interface: `id: string` (lowercase, matching `/^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/`), `name: string`, `description: string`, `isHeartbeat: boolean`, `properties: EntityProperty[]`, `activities: ProductActivity[]`
     - `CustomerEntity` interface: `name: string`, `properties: EntityProperty[]`, `activities: CustomerActivity[]`
     - `InteractionEntity` interface: `name: string`, `properties: EntityProperty[]`, `activities: InteractionActivity[]`
     - `EntityJsonSchema` interface: `entityName: string`, `perspective: Perspective`, `schema: Record<string, unknown>`
   - Note: References `EntityProperty`, `ProductActivity`, `CustomerActivity`, `InteractionActivity` from S001 (must be defined first in the same file)

2. **Add Zod schemas for entity types**
   - File: `packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts`
   - Change: After the existing `EntityDefinitionSchema` (~line 63), add:
     - `ProductEntitySchema`: uses `EntityPropertySchema` for properties array, `ProductActivitySchema` for activities array, `z.string().min(1).regex(/^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/)` for id
     - `CustomerEntitySchema`: uses `EntityPropertySchema` for properties, `CustomerActivitySchema` for activities
     - `InteractionEntitySchema`: uses `EntityPropertySchema` for properties, `InteractionActivitySchema` for activities
     - `EntityJsonSchemaSchema`: uses `PerspectiveSchema` for perspective, `z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())` for schema
   - Note: References `EntityPropertySchema`, `ProductActivitySchema`, `CustomerActivitySchema`, `InteractionActivitySchema` from S001

3. **Add type-level tests for entity types**
   - File: `packages/core/src/types/__tests__/outputs.test.ts`
   - Change: Add 4 new `describe` blocks after the existing "EntityPropertyDef and EntityDefinition" tests (~line 131):
     - `ProductEntity`: construct a valid ProductEntity with nested EntityProperty[] and ProductActivity[], verify fields
     - `CustomerEntity`: construct with nested EntityProperty[] and CustomerActivity[], verify fields
     - `InteractionEntity`: construct with nested EntityProperty[] and InteractionActivity[], verify fields
     - `EntityJsonSchema`: construct with entityName, perspective, and schema object, verify fields
   - Each test should verify the nested structure (activities containing properties_supported referencing entity property names)

4. **Add Zod schema validation tests**
   - File: `packages/core/src/schema/__tests__/outputs.test.ts`
   - Change: Add 4 new `describe` blocks with valid fixtures and positive/negative test cases:
     - `ProductEntitySchema`: test valid entity with nested activities and properties, reject missing id, reject invalid id format (uppercase), reject missing activities array
     - `CustomerEntitySchema`: test valid entity, reject missing name, reject missing activities
     - `InteractionEntitySchema`: test valid entity, reject missing name, reject missing properties
     - `EntityJsonSchemaSchema`: test valid schema, reject missing entityName, reject invalid perspective value, test that arbitrary schema object passes

### Test Plan
- [ ] Verify ProductEntity type compiles: create a ProductEntity literal in `types/__tests__/outputs.test.ts` with nested ProductActivity containing `properties_supported` referencing entity property names — test passes
- [ ] Verify CustomerEntity type compiles: create a CustomerEntity literal with nested CustomerActivity containing `derivation_rule` — test passes
- [ ] Verify InteractionEntity type compiles: create an InteractionEntity literal with nested InteractionActivity — test passes
- [ ] Verify EntityJsonSchema type compiles: create an EntityJsonSchema literal with `perspective: "product"` and arbitrary schema — test passes
- [ ] Verify `ProductEntitySchema.safeParse(validProductEntity).success` returns `true` for a complete valid object
- [ ] Verify `ProductEntitySchema.safeParse({...validProductEntity, id: "UPPER"}).success` returns `false` (id must be lowercase)
- [ ] Verify `CustomerEntitySchema.safeParse(validCustomerEntity).success` returns `true`
- [ ] Verify `CustomerEntitySchema.safeParse({...missing name}).success` returns `false`
- [ ] Verify `InteractionEntitySchema.safeParse(validInteractionEntity).success` returns `true`
- [ ] Verify `EntityJsonSchemaSchema.safeParse(validEntityJsonSchema).success` returns `true`
- [ ] Verify `EntityJsonSchemaSchema.safeParse({...invalid perspective}).success` returns `false`
- [ ] Run `npm test` in `packages/core` — exits with code 0

---

title: Infer entity model from product content
description:
## Context

**Mission M001:** Validate MCP-first product profile generation from website crawling
**Epic M001-E001:** Core Analysis Engine - LLM extraction into product profile
**Story M001-E001-S003:** Infer core entities, types, properties, and relationships from features, pricing, and about pages.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-04-extract-entity-model-design.md

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** `docs/plans/2026-02-04-extract-entity-model-impl.md`

### Overview
Single-pass LLM extraction using Claude Haiku. Fetches crawled pages (features, pricing, homepage, about), sends labeled sections to Haiku with entity type definitions and relationship rules, stores results via updateSectionInternal. Follows the S001 extractor pattern.

### Tasks
4 TDD tasks covering:
- Add `listByProductInternal` internalQuery to crawledPages (prerequisite shared with S001)
- Add `updateSectionInternal` and `getInternal` internal helpers to productProfiles (prerequisite shared with S001)
- Create `extractEntities` internalAction with full test coverage (core task)
- Verify full test suite passes with no regressions

### Testing
- [ ] Unit tests for internal helpers (convex-test, no auth)
- [ ] Unit tests for extractor with mocked Anthropic SDK
- [ ] Edge cases: no pages, code-block JSON, partial page types, evidence field stripping
- [ ] Run project test suite to verify changes

title: Extract product outcomes from marketing content
description:
## Context

**Mission M001:** Validate MCP-first product profile generation from website crawling
**Epic M001-E001:** Core Analysis Engine - LLM extraction into product profile

**Story M001-E001-S005:** Extract outcomes/jobs-to-be-done from hero messaging, value props, and testimonials.

## Specification

A Convex action analyzes homepage hero messaging, value propositions, and customer testimonials to identify the outcomes the product delivers — primary and secondary jobs-to-be-done, linked to features where possible.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] Outcomes extracted as array of { description, type, linkedFeatures[] } — type is primary/secondary/tertiary
2. [unit] Primary outcome captures the main job-to-be-done (e.g., 'collaborate visually in real-time')
3. [unit] Outcomes are linked to specific features mentioned on the site where possible
4. [unit] Evidence includes URLs and excerpts from hero sections, value props, testimonials
5. [integration] Result stored via updateSection(productId, 'outcomes', data) matching schema

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Classify outcome type by positioning hierarchy (hero=primary, value-props=secondary, testimonial-only=tertiary)
- Reflects company's own prioritization; more objective than business impact inference
- Product leaders want clean positioning data to make their own judgments

### Technical Perspective
- Single Haiku call with labeled page sections (homepage, features, customers)
- Follows established S001-S004 pattern: internalAction → listByProductInternal → Haiku → updateSectionInternal
- Feature linkage as string array from marketing copy, not structured entity reference
- ~25KB truncation per page, flat evidence array

### Simplification Review
- Tightened outcome type definitions to one line each
- Confirmed single-call approach sufficient for surface-level marketing copy
- No need to read existing profile sections (unlike S004)

### Final Design
Single internalAction with one Haiku call. Fetches homepage/features/customers pages, extracts outcomes classified by positioning hierarchy, links to feature names from marketing copy, stores via updateSectionInternal.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-04-extract-product-outcomes-design.md

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** docs/plans/2026-02-04-extract-product-outcomes-impl.md

### Overview
Single internalAction with one Claude Haiku call. Fetches crawled homepage/features/customers pages, extracts outcomes classified by positioning hierarchy, links to feature names, stores via updateSectionInternal.

### Tasks
4 TDD tasks covering:
- Add shared internal helpers (listByProductInternal, createInternal, updateSectionInternal) if missing
- Write failing tests for extractOutcomes (7 test cases covering all acceptance criteria)
- Implement extractOutcomes internalAction with prompt, JSON parsing, evidence stripping
- Verify types, lint, and full test suite

### Testing
- [ ] Run project test suite to verify changes

---

title: M006-E004-S003: Update MeasurementSpecSection UI for entity-grouped events
description:
## Story: M006-E004-S003

Add entity-grouped view to MeasurementSpecSection. Entity cards with name, description, property table, and nested events. Toggle between entity and category views. Category view is fallback for legacy data.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Entity view rendered by default when entities exist
- Each entity renders as card with name, description, event count badge
- Entity card includes property table (name, type, required, description)
- Events nested under parent entity using existing EventRow component
- Toggle between 'By Entity' and 'By Category' views
- Category view unchanged from current implementation
- Legacy data (no entities) shows category view, toggle hidden
- Summary bar shows entity count alongside event count
- Events without entity_id shown in 'Ungrouped' section

### Key Files
- src/components/product-profile/MeasurementSpecSection.tsx
- convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Toggle right-aligned on same line as summary bar — perspective shift on same dataset, not navigation
- Confidence score stays visible in both views — trust in data, not grouping
- Summary bar shows entity count alongside event count when entities present
- The magic moment is the seamless perspective shift when toggling between entity and category views

### Technical Perspective
- Simple two-button toggle (not Radix Tabs) — different mental model from page-level tab navigation
- ~10 lines inline JSX: bg-gray-100 container, white + shadow-sm for active state
- Always-visible property table — entity properties define what an entity *is*, hiding behind collapse contradicts entity-first mental model
- Reuse EventRow as-is in both views — visual consistency, no modifications needed
- Inline event filtering (simple filter() calls) rather than Map-based helper

### Simplification Review
- Removed separate component extractions (CategorySummaryBar, EntitySummaryBar, ViewToggle) — inline as JSX
- Removed Phase 1 refactoring — build entity view directly
- Kept always-visible property table (core to entity-first mental model)
- Kept Required column in property table (explicit acceptance criterion)
- Kept entity count in summary bar (explicit acceptance criterion)
- Inline event grouping logic — simple filter() calls rather than Map-based helper
- Entity cards stay as local function for readability

### Final Design
Entity cards with always-visible property schemas and nested EventRows. Inline two-button toggle on summary bar for view switching. Confidence visible in both views. Legacy fallback to category view. All in MeasurementSpecSection.tsx.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-12-measurement-spec-entity-ui-design.md

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: viewMode state, entity card rendering, property table, event filtering per entity, toggle, summary bar updates, ungrouped section, tests (9 cases)

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

title: Implement extractActivationLevels internalAction
description:
## Context

**Mission M002:** Discover and model multi-level activation from product content
**Epic M002-E003:** Multi-Level Activation Extraction

**Story M002-E003-S004:** Implement extractActivationLevels internalAction

**Dependencies:** Requires S001 (types), S002 (prompt), S003 (filtering) to be complete.

## Specification

An internalAction fetches crawled pages, filters to activation-relevant content,
calls Claude Haiku with the activation prompt, parses the response, and stores
the multi-level activation data on the product profile.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] extractActivationLevels is exported as internalAction with productId arg
2. [unit] Action fetches crawled pages via internal.crawledPages.listByProductInternal
3. [unit] Action filters pages using filterActivationPages
4. [unit] Action calls Claude Haiku with ACTIVATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT
5. [unit] Response is parsed via parseActivationLevelsResponse function
6. [unit] Parsed result is stored via updateSectionInternal with section='definitions'
7. [integration] Running extractor on Miro product produces 3-4 activation levels
8. [integration] Stored activation includes levels array with primaryActivation set

## Guidance

Follow the exact pattern from extractIdentity.ts.

Note: Section is "definitions" not "activation" - we're updating definitions.activation.

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/extractIdentity.ts
- convex/analysis/extractJourney.ts

---

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** `docs/plans/2026-02-05-extract-activation-levels-impl.md`

### Overview
Implement extractActivationLevels as an internalAction following the extractIdentity.ts pattern. The action fetches crawled pages, filters to activation-relevant types, calls Claude Haiku, parses the response, and stores in definitions.activation while preserving other definition fields.

### Tasks
7 TDD tasks covering:
- Types (SignalStrength, ActivationCriterion, ActivationLevel, ActivationLevelsResult)
- ACTIVATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT constant
- parseActivationLevelsResponse parser with validation
- filterActivationPages and buildActivationPageContext helpers
- extractActivationLevels internalAction
- Integration tests with mocked Claude responses
- Full test suite verification

### Testing
- [ ] Run project test suite to verify changes

title: Add public test action for activation map generation
description:
## Context

**Mission M004:** Actionable Output Generation
**Epic M004-E003:** Activation Map Generation

**Story M004-E003-S003:** Add public test action for activation map generation

**Dependencies:** Requires M004-E003-S002 (activation map generator)

## Specification

A public testGenerateActivationMap action allows manual testing of activation map generation via Convex dashboard, with results logged and returned.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] testGenerateActivationMap public action accepts productId
2. [unit] Action calls generateActivationMap and returns full result
3. [unit] Result includes execution_time_ms and stage count
4. [integration] Action callable from Convex dashboard
5. [integration] Linear test via dashboard produces valid ActivationMap

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/lenses/orchestrate.ts

---
*Product Story: M004-E003-S003 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Use Pattern B (enriched wrapper) with action-level timing, since generateActivationMap returns a business artifact, not a telemetry wrapper
- Co-locate test action in same file as the internal action (convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts)
- Spread result with ...result to stay resilient to ActivationMap field changes
- Add stage_count as convenience field for quick dashboard inspection

### Simplification Review
- Design approved as minimal - nothing to remove or simplify
- Pattern consistency with existing test actions (testRunAllLenses, testExtractActivation)

### Final Design
Add testGenerateActivationMap public action to convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts that wraps the internal action with timing and stage_count metadata. ~15 lines of code following established patterns.

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: adding the public action export, unit test with mocked internal action, integration test once S002 lands

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

## Implementation Plan

### Summary

Add a public `testGenerateActivationMap` action to `convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts` that wraps the internal `generateActivationMap` action with action-level timing and a `stage_count` convenience field. This enables manual testing and debugging of activation map generation via the Convex dashboard. A companion test file validates the enriched return structure and timing behavior.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateActivationMap.ts` exports a public `testGenerateActivationMap` action with args `{ productId: v.id("products") }`
- [ ] AC2: The action calls `internal.analysis.outputs.generateActivationMap.generateActivationMap` via `ctx.runAction` and spreads the full result into the return value
- [ ] AC3: The return value includes `execution_time_ms` (number > 0) and `stage_count` (equal to `result.stages.length`)
- [ ] AC4: The action is exported with `action({...})` (not `internalAction`), making it visible in the Convex dashboard Functions panel

   - Pattern reference: `convex/analysis/lenses/orchestrate.ts:156-166` (testRunAllLenses) for the thin wrapper structure, enhanced with timing per design doc `docs/plans/2026-02-08-activation-map-test-action-design.md`

title: M006-E001-S001: Replace TF-IDF clustering with LLM-based semantic clustering
description:
## Story: M006-E001-S001

Replace clusterCandidatesCore (TF-IDF cosine similarity) with an LLM-based clustering function that sends all validated candidates to Claude for semantic grouping. Keep TF-IDF as fallback.

### Acceptance Criteria
- New clusterCandidatesLLM function accepts ValidatedCandidate[] and returns CandidateCluster[]
- No cluster contains two candidates from the same lens
- Parsing handles JSON in code fences and raw JSON
- Fallback to TF-IDF clustering if LLM call fails
- Prompt includes same-lens constraint as explicit rule
- Prompt requests 15-30 clusters as target range
- runConvergencePipeline uses LLM clustering instead of TF-IDF by default

### Key Files
- convex/analysis/convergence/clusterCandidates.ts (add clusterCandidatesLLM)
- convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts (wire new clustering)
- convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Single-call approach is simplest: ~70 candidates fits in one Claude call (~2,050 input tokens)
- Per-lens clustering + merge rejected: 7 LLM calls + merge logic adds code/latency/failure modes for no benefit at this scale
- Post-hoc same-lens validation with repair (split violations into singletons) -- pragmatic, 3 lines of code
- Reuse Anthropic client already created in runConvergencePipeline -- just move creation earlier
- Use extractJson from shared.ts for JSON parsing consistency

### Simplification Review
- Design approved as minimal and inevitable
- Repair strategy justified: rare edge case, trivial code, more resilient than retry loops
- No hidden bloat -- single-call architecture sidesteps per-lens coordination complexity

### Final Design
Four new exports in clusterCandidates.ts: CLUSTERING_SYSTEM_PROMPT, buildClusteringPrompt, parseClusteringResponse, clusterCandidatesLLM. Wire into convergeAndTier.ts with try/catch: LLM first, TF-IDF fallback. Same-lens repair + orphan handling for resilience. No changes to types.ts.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-12-llm-semantic-clustering-design.md

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: system prompt text, buildClusteringPrompt format, parseClusteringResponse validation/repair logic, clusterCandidatesLLM function, convergeAndTier.ts wiring changes, test cases for parsing/repair/orphans/fallback

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

Implementation plan: docs/plans/2026-02-12-llm-semantic-clustering-impl.md

title: Implement convergence and tiering
description:
## Context

**Mission M003:** 7-Lens Value Discovery Validation
**Epic M003-E002:** Validation & Convergence - Clean and cluster lens output

**Story M003-E002-S004:** Merge clusters into named value moments with tier assignment.

**Dependencies:** Requires S003 (clustering) to exist.

## Specification

An LLM-assisted convergence function merges each cluster into a single named
value moment, combining insights from all contributing lenses. Moments are
tiered by lens count: 5+ = Tier 1, 3-4 = Tier 2, 1-2 = Tier 3.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] convergeAndTier function accepts CandidateCluster[] and returns ValueMoment[]
2. [unit] Each cluster is merged into a single ValueMoment using LLM
3. [unit] Value moment name follows format: verb + what user gets
4. [unit] Clusters with 5+ lenses are assigned Tier 1
5. [unit] Clusters with 3-4 lenses are assigned Tier 2
6. [unit] Clusters with 1-2 lenses are assigned Tier 3
7. [integration] Linear produces 4-6 Tier 1 moments
8. [integration] Total value moments for Linear is 15-30
9. [integration] Merged descriptions combine insights from all contributing lenses
10. [integration] Each moment has contributing_candidates for traceability

## Guidance

Create convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts as an internalAction.

LLM prompt merges candidate insights into single named moment with verb + outcome format.

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts
- convex/analysis/convergence/clusterCandidates.ts

---
*Product Story: M003-E002-S004 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Trust upstream clustering (0.7 similarity threshold) — no split logic for V1
- Log warnings if LLM flags incoherent clusters, but always merge
- "Verb + outcome" naming is the key user-facing quality signal
- Ship and iterate: add split handling only if real data shows it's needed

### Technical Perspective
- Per-cluster LLM calls with Promise.allSettled — matches orchestrate.ts patterns
- Each cluster is a unit of work: merge it, tier it, store it
- Pure functions for testability: assignTier, parseMergeResponse
- Fallback to directMerge on LLM failure to avoid data loss
- Model: claude-sonnet (merge quality needs judgment)

### Simplification Review
- Removed tier-based merge branching: call LLM uniformly for all clusters
- Removed testRunConvergencePipeline: premature API surface for unwired code
- Inlined buildMergePrompt and dedupeStrings: no abstractions for one-time use
- Kept assignTier and parseMergeResponse as pure functions (genuine testing value)

### Final Design
convergeAndTier(clusters, client) iterates all clusters uniformly via Promise.allSettled, calls Claude Sonnet per cluster with verb+outcome prompt, parses JSON response, assigns tier by lens count, falls back to directMerge on failure. runConvergencePipeline internalAction orchestrates validate → cluster → converge → store.

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover: pure functions with tests, LLM merge prompt, convergeAndTier core function, runConvergencePipeline internalAction.

**Design doc:** docs/plans/2026-02-07-convergence-and-tiering-design.md

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Implement `convergeAndTier.ts` — an LLM-assisted convergence function that merges each `CandidateCluster` into a named `ValueMoment` with tier assignment (5+ lenses = Tier 1, 3-4 = Tier 2, 1-2 = Tier 3). The function calls Claude Sonnet per cluster via `Promise.allSettled`, parses verb+outcome named moments, and falls back to `directMerge` on LLM failure. A `runConvergencePipeline` internalAction orchestrates the full validate → cluster → converge → store flow.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AC1: `convergeAndTier` function in `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts` accepts `CandidateCluster[]` and an Anthropic client, returns `ValueMoment[]`
- [ ] AC2: Each cluster triggers one LLM call to `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` via the Anthropic client (verifiable via mocked client call count in tests)
- [ ] AC3: Each returned `ValueMoment.name` starts with a verb (e.g., "Gain", "Reduce", "Accelerate") — validated by `parseMergeResponse` throwing on non-verb names
- [ ] AC4: `assignTier(5)` returns `1`, `assignTier(7)` returns `1`
- [ ] AC5: `assignTier(3)` returns `2`, `assignTier(4)` returns `2`
- [ ] AC6: `assignTier(1)` returns `3`, `assignTier(2)` returns `3`
- [ ] AC7: (Integration — deferred) Linear produces 4-6 Tier 1 moments when full pipeline is wired
- [ ] AC8: (Integration — deferred) Total value moments for Linear is 15-30
- [ ] AC9: Each `ValueMoment.description` contains text referencing contributing lenses (verifiable in unit test with mocked LLM returning multi-lens description)
- [ ] AC10: Each `ValueMoment.contributing_candidates` array contains the `id` values of all candidates in the source cluster

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Create `convergeAndTier.ts` with pure functions**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts`
   - Export `assignTier(lens_count: number): ValueMomentTier` — pure tier mapping: `>=5 → 1`, `>=3 → 2`, else `3`
   - Export `parseMergeResponse(text: string): { name: string; description: string; roles: string[]; product_surfaces: string[]; is_coherent: boolean }` — extract JSON from code fences (regex: `/\`\`\`(?:json)?\s*\n?([\s\S]*?)\n?\s*\`\`\`/`), validate required fields (`name`, `description`, `roles`, `product_surfaces`, `is_coherent`), throw on missing fields. Follow `parseIdentityResponse` pattern from `convex/analysis/extractIdentity.ts`

2. **Add `directMerge` fallback function**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts`
   - Export `directMerge(cluster: CandidateCluster): ValueMoment` — constructs a ValueMoment without LLM by: concatenating candidate names with " / " as the moment name prefixed with "Achieve" (verb), joining descriptions, deduping roles and product_surfaces via `Set`, setting tier via `assignTier(cluster.lens_count)`, collecting `contributing_candidates` from `cluster.candidates.map(c => c.id)`

3. **Add `MERGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT` and core `convergeAndTier` function**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts`
   - `MERGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT`: instruct Claude to merge candidates into one named value moment, name MUST start with verb (Gain, Reduce, Accelerate, etc.), combine insights from ALL lenses, return JSON in code fences with fields: `name`, `description`, `roles`, `product_surfaces`, `is_coherent`
   - Export async `convergeAndTier(clusters: CandidateCluster[], client: Anthropic): Promise<ValueMoment[]>` with Promise.allSettled pattern, parseMergeResponse + assignTier per result, directMerge fallback on failure
   - Use model `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` and `max_tokens: 4096` matching orchestrate.ts pattern

4. **Add `runConvergencePipeline` internalAction**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.ts`
   - Orchestrates: instantiate Anthropic client → cluster candidates → converge and tier → build ConvergenceResult → store via updateSectionInternal

5. **Write unit tests for pure functions**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.test.ts`
   - Tests for `assignTier` (AC4, AC5, AC6), `parseMergeResponse` (AC3), `directMerge` (AC10)

6. **Write unit tests for `convergeAndTier` with mocked Anthropic client**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.test.ts`
   - Tests for AC1 (input/output types), AC2 (one LLM call per cluster), AC9 (lens descriptions), AC10 (contributing_candidates), fallback behavior

### Test Plan
- [ ] `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/convergence/convergeAndTier.test.ts` passes with all tests green
- [ ] `assignTier` tests verify AC4, AC5, AC6
- [ ] `parseMergeResponse` tests verify JSON extraction from code fences and field validation
- [ ] `convergeAndTier` mock tests verify AC1, AC2, AC9, AC10
- [ ] `directMerge` tests verify fallback produces valid ValueMoment with verb-prefixed name
- [ ] AC7 and AC8 (integration) deferred until full pipeline wired
- [ ] Existing tests still pass: `npm run test:run` exits with code 0

---
*Plan created via /plan-issue*

title: Infer journey stages and draft lifecycle definitions
description:
## Context

**Mission M001:** Validate MCP-first product profile generation from website crawling
**Epic M001-E001:** Core Analysis Engine - LLM extraction into product profile

**Story M001-E001-S004:** Infer user journey stages and draft activation/firstValue/active/atRisk/churn definitions from crawled content.

## Specification

A Convex action analyzes crawled content to propose journey stages (signup → activated → active → paying → retained) customized to the product, and drafts definitions for activation, firstValue, active, atRisk, and churn — each with reasoning and evidence.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] Journey stages extracted as array of { name, description, order } with product-specific naming
2. [unit] Activation definition drafted with criteria[], timeWindow, reasoning, and evidence
3. [unit] First Value definition drafted with description, criteria[], reasoning, and evidence
4. [unit] Active, atRisk, and churn definitions drafted with same structure
5. [unit] Each definition has source field set to 'ai-inferred' to distinguish from user-validated
6. [unit] Confidence scores are medium-low (0.3-0.5) reflecting these are drafts needing user validation
7. [integration] Journey stored via updateSection(productId, 'journey', data)
8. [integration] Definitions stored via updateSection(productId, 'definitions', data) with all sub-keys

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** `docs/plans/2026-02-04-extract-journey-definitions-impl.md`

### Overview
Single internalAction with two sequential Claude Haiku calls — Call 1 extracts observable journey stages (higher confidence), Call 2 infers lifecycle definitions with source='ai-inferred' (lower confidence). Journey persisted before definitions call for partial success safety.

### Tasks
4 TDD tasks covering:
- Add shared internal helpers (listByProductInternal, createInternal, updateSectionInternal, getInternal)
- Write 11 failing tests for extractJourney
- Implement extractJourney internalAction with two-call architecture
- Verify types, lint, full test suite

### Testing
- [ ] Run project test suite to verify changes

---
*Plan created via headless session*

## Guidance

- Create convex/analysis/extractJourney.ts as an internalAction
- Two sequential LLM calls: journey stages first, definitions second
- Input: homepage, features, about, pricing pages
- Use business model type (if identity already extracted) to inform defaults
- Journey schema: { stages: [{ name, description, order }], confidence, evidence[] }
- Definitions schema: { activation: { criteria[], timeWindow?, reasoning, confidence, source, evidence[] }, firstValue: { description, criteria[], reasoning, confidence, source, evidence[] }, active: {...}, atRisk: {...}, churn: {...} }
- Set source = 'ai-inferred' for all definitions
- These are explicitly drafts — prompt should explain it's proposing starting points

### Reference Files
- convex/productProfiles.ts
- convex/schema.ts (lines 491-543 for journey + definitions schema)

---

title: M012-E002-S003: Response Parser and Validator
description:
Mission: M012 - Double Three-Layer Measurement Spec
Epic: E002 - LLM Prompt, Parser, and Validator
Story: S003 - Response Parser and Validator

## What
Rewrite parseMeasurementSpecResponse for the new perspective-grouped structure with cross-reference validation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Accepts valid new-format JSON and returns MeasurementSpec
- Validates: perspectives object with customer, product, interaction keys
- Validates: product entities non-empty, exactly one isHeartbeat: true
- Validates: product entity id matches /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/
- Validates: properties_supported entries reference actual entity property names
- Validates: customer entity has derivation_rule on each activity
- Validates: interaction entity has activities
- Validates: confidence 0-1
- Warns (warnings[], not throw) when product entity count > 7
- Throws descriptive errors for: missing perspectives, invalid entity id, heartbeat count != 1, properties_supported mismatch, missing derivation_rule
- Initializes jsonSchemas as [] (E003 populates it after parsing)
- Tests for: valid parse, missing perspectives, invalid id, no heartbeat, multiple heartbeats, properties_supported mismatch, entity count warning, missing derivation_rule

## Files to Modify
- packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/measurement-spec.ts
- packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/__tests__/outputs/measurement-spec.test.ts

## Context
Cross-reference validation is the most important new check: for each activity, verify properties_supported entries exist as entity property names. Parser no longer computes coverage/total_events. jsonSchemas populated by E003 after parsing.

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Rewrite `parseMeasurementSpecResponse` to parse the new perspective-grouped LLM response format into the new `MeasurementSpec` type. The current parser validates flat `entities[]` + `events[]` + `userStateModel[]`; the new parser validates `perspectives.{product,customer,interaction}` with nested entities and activities. The key new validation is cross-referencing `properties_supported` entries against actual entity property names. Remove old validation constants (`EVENT_NAME_RE`, `VALID_CATEGORIES`) and coverage computation code that no longer apply.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `parseMeasurementSpecResponse` accepts a valid JSON string with `perspectives.product.entities`, `perspectives.customer.entities`, `perspectives.interaction.entities`, `confidence`, and returns a `MeasurementSpec` object with `jsonSchemas: []` and `sources: []`
- [ ] Throws `"Missing required field: perspectives"` when input JSON lacks a `perspectives` object
- [ ] Throws `"Missing required field: perspectives.product"` when `perspectives.product` is missing
- [ ] Throws `"Missing required field: perspectives.customer"` when `perspectives.customer` is missing
- [ ] Throws `"Missing required field: perspectives.interaction"` when `perspectives.interaction` is missing
- [ ] Throws `"Product entities must be a non-empty array"` when `perspectives.product.entities` is empty or missing
- [ ] Throws error containing `"id must match"` when a product entity id fails `/^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/`
- [ ] Throws error containing `"none found"` when no product entity has `isHeartbeat: true`
- [ ] Throws error containing `"found 2"` (or count) when multiple product entities have `isHeartbeat: true`
- [ ] Throws error containing `"properties_supported references"` when a ProductActivity's `properties_supported` entry doesn't match any property name on its parent entity
- [ ] Throws error containing `"derivation_rule"` when a CustomerActivity lacks `derivation_rule`
- [ ] Throws error when interaction entity has zero activities
- [ ] Throws `"confidence must be between 0 and 1"` when confidence is out of range
- [ ] Adds `"Product entity count (N) exceeds recommended maximum of 7"` to `warnings[]` but does NOT throw when entity count > 7
- [ ] Old validation constants `EVENT_NAME_RE` and `VALID_CATEGORIES` are removed
- [ ] Coverage computation code (activation_levels_covered, value_moments_covered, perspective_distribution) is removed
- [ ] `npm test -- --run` in `packages/mcp-server` exits with code 0

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Remove old validation constants and coverage code**
   - File: `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/measurement-spec.ts`
   - Remove `EVENT_NAME_RE` constant (line 300)
   - Remove `VALID_CATEGORIES` constant (line 302)
   - Keep `ENTITY_ID_RE` (still needed for product entity id validation)
   - Keep `VALID_PERSPECTIVES` — remove it too (no longer validating per-event perspectives; perspectives are structural keys now)

2. **Rewrite `parseMeasurementSpecResponse` function**
   - File: `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/measurement-spec.ts`
   - Replace the entire function body (lines 306-433) with new validation logic:
   
   **Step 1: Extract JSON and validate top-level structure**
   - Use `extractJson(responseText)` (unchanged)
   - Validate `parsed.perspectives` is an object
   - Validate `parsed.perspectives.product`, `.customer`, `.interaction` exist
   - Validate `parsed.confidence` is number between 0 and 1
   
   **Step 2: Validate product entities**
   - `perspectives.product.entities` must be non-empty array
   - Each product entity: validate `id` matches `ENTITY_ID_RE`
   - Exactly one entity must have `isHeartbeat: true`
   - Each entity must have `activities` array (at least 1)
   - **Cross-reference validation**: for each activity, each entry in `properties_supported` must exist in the entity's `properties[].name` set
   - If entity count > 7, add warning to `warnings[]` (don't throw)
   
   **Step 3: Validate customer entities**
   - `perspectives.customer.entities` must be array (can be non-empty)
   - Each customer entity: each activity must have `derivation_rule` (non-empty string)
   
   **Step 4: Validate interaction entities**
   - `perspectives.interaction.entities` must be array
   - Each interaction entity must have `activities` (at least 1)
   
   **Step 5: Return MeasurementSpec**
   - Return `{ perspectives: parsed.perspectives, jsonSchemas: [], confidence, sources: [], warnings }` (warnings only if non-empty)

3. **Update test helper `makeValidSpecJson`**
   - File: `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/__tests__/outputs/measurement-spec.test.ts`
   - Replace `makeValidSpecJson` to produce new-format JSON with `perspectives` structure
   - Default valid fixture: a Miro-like product with Board entity (heartbeat), one ProductActivity with properties_supported, one CustomerEntity with derivation_rule, one InteractionEntity with activities

4. **Rewrite parser test suite `parseMeasurementSpecResponse`**
   - File: `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/__tests__/outputs/measurement-spec.test.ts`
   - Remove old tests: "parses valid measurement spec JSON" (references events/coverage), "computes coverage from events", "rejects missing entities", "rejects missing events", "rejects event with invalid entity_id", "rejects missing confidence" (rewrite), "rejects confidence out of range" (keep)
   - Remove old validation test suites: "event name format validation", "perspective enum validation", "category enum validation" (these no longer apply — events don't exist)
   - Keep: "entity ID format validation" tests (still validate product entity IDs)
   - Keep: "heartbeat uniqueness validation" tests (update to use new fixture format)
   
   **New tests to add:**
   - `describe("parseMeasurementSpecResponse")`:
     - `it("parses valid new-format measurement spec")` — full Miro-like fixture, asserts perspectives structure, jsonSchemas: [], sources: [], confidence
     - `it("rejects missing perspectives")` — input without perspectives key
     - `it("rejects missing perspectives.product")` — perspectives without product
     - `it("rejects missing perspectives.customer")`
     - `it("rejects missing perspectives.interaction")`
     - `it("rejects empty product entities")` — perspectives.product.entities: []
   - `describe("parseMeasurementSpecResponse — properties_supported cross-reference")`:
     - `it("rejects properties_supported referencing non-existent property")` — activity has properties_supported: ["nonexistent"], entity has properties: [{name: "board_id"}]
     - `it("accepts valid properties_supported references")` — all properties_supported entries match entity property names
   - `describe("parseMeasurementSpecResponse — customer derivation_rule")`:
     - `it("rejects customer activity without derivation_rule")` — customer activity missing derivation_rule field
     - `it("rejects customer activity with empty derivation_rule")` — derivation_rule: ""
     - `it("accepts valid customer activity with derivation_rule")` — derivation_rule present
   - `describe("parseMeasurementSpecResponse — interaction activities")`:
     - `it("rejects interaction entity with no activities")` — activities: []
   - `describe("parseMeasurementSpecResponse — entity count warning")`:
     - `it("warns when product entity count exceeds 7")` — 8 entities, assert result.warnings contains message, does not throw
     - `it("no warning when product entity count is 7 or fewer")` — 3 entities, assert no warnings

5. **Remove stale test suites that reference old format**
   - File: `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/__tests__/outputs/measurement-spec.test.ts`
   - Remove `describe("parseMeasurementSpecResponse — event name format validation")` (lines 557-629)
   - Remove `describe("parseMeasurementSpecResponse — perspective enum validation")` (lines 631-655)
   - Remove `describe("parseMeasurementSpecResponse — category enum validation")` (lines 657-681)
   - Remove `describe("MEASUREMENT_SPEC_SYSTEM_PROMPT — softened Step 3")` (lines 683-705) — system prompt is rewritten by S001

### Test Plan
- [ ] Valid Miro-like input: `parseMeasurementSpecResponse(miroJson)` returns `MeasurementSpec` with `perspectives.product.entities.length >= 1`, `jsonSchemas` as `[]`, `confidence` as number, `sources` as `[]`
- [ ] Missing perspectives: `parseMeasurementSpecResponse('{"confidence":0.7}')` throws "Missing required field: perspectives"
- [ ] Missing product: perspectives without `product` key throws
- [ ] Empty product entities: `perspectives.product.entities: []` throws "non-empty"
- [ ] Invalid entity id: product entity with `id: "1board"` throws "id must match"
- [ ] No heartbeat: all product entities have `isHeartbeat: false` throws "none found"
- [ ] Multiple heartbeats: two product entities with `isHeartbeat: true` throws "found 2"
- [ ] Properties_supported mismatch: product activity with `properties_supported: ["nonexistent"]` on entity with `properties: [{name: "board_id"}]` throws "properties_supported references"
- [ ] Missing derivation_rule: customer activity without `derivation_rule` throws
- [ ] Empty interaction activities: interaction entity with `activities: []` throws
- [ ] Entity count > 7: 8 product entities — returns successfully with `warnings` containing count message
- [ ] Entity count <= 7: 3 product entities — `warnings` is undefined or empty
- [ ] Confidence out of range: `confidence: 1.5` throws "confidence must be between 0 and 1"
- [ ] `npm test -- --run` in `packages/mcp-server` exits with code 0 and all tests pass

---

title: Type scan profile object properly — remove as any casts
description:
## Problem
`scan.ts` builds the profile as `Record<string, unknown>` and uses three `as any` casts (lines 136, 193, 198) when passing to `storage.save()`, `formatOutput()`, and `writeOutputFile()`. No compile-time validation that all expected fields are present.

## Context
Discovered during retro after M011 (scan output quality).

## Solution
Define a `ScanProfileOutput` type that reflects what `runScan()` actually builds. Type `profile` with it. Create a transformation function if the storage/formatter interfaces differ.

## Files
- `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` (lines 136-137, 193-194, 198-199)

---
*Priority: Medium | Effort: Medium*
*Created via /retro*

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Replace the untyped `Record<string, unknown>` profile construction and three `as any` casts in `scan.ts` with proper `ProductProfile` typing from `@basesignal/storage`. The storage `ProductProfile` already has all the fields that scan sets (identity, metadata, completeness, overallConfidence, journey, metrics, outputs) plus an index signature for forward-compatible fields like `lifecycle_states`. This gives compile-time validation while remaining fully compatible with storage and formatter consumers.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` contains zero `as any` casts (verified by `grep 'as any' packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` returning no matches)
- [ ] `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` contains zero `eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` comments (verified by `grep 'no-explicit-any' packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` returning no matches)
- [ ] `profile` variable in `runScan()` is typed as `ProductProfile` instead of `Record<string, unknown>` (verified in `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` at line ~113)
- [ ] `npm run build` exits with code 0 (no TypeScript compilation errors)
- [ ] `npm test -- --run packages/cli/src/commands/scan.test.ts` exits with code 0 (all existing tests pass)

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Import `ProductProfile` type**
   - File: `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts`
   - Change: Add `import type { ProductProfile } from "@basesignal/storage";` alongside the existing dynamic `import("@basesignal/storage")` usage. Since `FileStorage` is imported dynamically (line 62), we add a static type-only import at the top for `ProductProfile`.

2. **Type the profile variable as `ProductProfile`**
   - File: `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts`
   - Change: Replace `const profile: Record<string, unknown> = {` (line 113) with `const profile: ProductProfile = {`
   - Also change `identity: pipelineResult.identity,` to `identity: pipelineResult.identity ?? undefined,` because `PipelineResult.identity` is `IdentityResult | null` but `ProductProfile.identity` is `{ ... } | undefined` — null is not assignable to the optional field type.

3. **Remove `as any` cast at `storage.save()` call**
   - File: `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts`
   - Change: Remove the `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` comment (line 136) and change `await storage.save(profile as any)` (line 137) to `await storage.save(profile)`.

4. **Remove `as any` cast at `formatOutput()` call**
   - File: `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts`
   - Change: Remove the `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` comment (line 193) and change `formatOutput(profile as any, options.format)` (line 194) to `formatOutput(profile, options.format)`.

5. **Remove `as any` cast at `writeOutputFile()` call**
   - File: `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts`
   - Change: Remove the `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` comment (line 198) and change `writeOutputFile(options.output, profile as any)` (line 199) to `writeOutputFile(options.output, profile)`.

6. **Handle potential type narrowing for conditional assignments**
   - File: `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts`
   - Change: The conditional assignments (`profile.journey`, `profile.metrics`, `profile.lifecycle_states`, `profile.outputs`) should work through the index signature. If `MeasurementSpec` or `PipelineOutputs` are not assignable to `Record<string, unknown>` (TypeScript interface constraint), use targeted narrowing like `as Record<string, unknown>` for those specific assignments (still much safer than the blanket `as any` on the entire profile object).

### Test Plan
- [ ] Run `grep 'as any' packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` — expect zero matches
- [ ] Run `grep 'no-explicit-any' packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` — expect zero matches
- [ ] Run `grep 'ProductProfile' packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` — expect matches for import and variable declaration
- [ ] Run `npm run build` — expect exit code 0
- [ ] Run `npm test -- --run packages/cli/src/commands/scan.test.ts` — expect all tests pass (9 tests in 2 suites)

---
*Plan created via /plan-issue*

title: Populate sources field in MeasurementSpec from crawled page URLs
description:
## Problem
`parseMeasurementSpecResponse` in measurement-spec.ts (line 493) hardcodes `sources: []`. Other outputs like activation-map and lifecycle-states extract sources from the LLM response. The measurement spec should similarly track which pages informed the analysis.

## Context
Discovered during retro after M012 implementation. Real scan of amplitude.com returned `sources: []` despite being based on 30 crawled pages.

## Solution
Either (a) pass page URLs from `assembleMeasurementInput` into the result directly, or (b) add a `sources` instruction to the LLM prompt and extract it from the response. Option (a) is simpler and more reliable.

## Files
- `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/measurement-spec.ts` (lines ~490-495, assembleMeasurementInput)

---
*Priority: Low | Effort: Small*
*Created via /retro*

title: [M013-E001-S002] Product List Page
description:
## Story: M013-E001-S002 — Product List Page
**Mission:** M013 — View Command — Browser-Based Profile Viewing
**Epic:** M013-E001 — View Command & HTTP Server

## Outcome
GET / on the view server returns an HTML page listing all scanned products. For each product, the list shows: product name, URL, scan date, and completeness percentage. Each row links to /{slug} for the product report. When no products have been scanned, a helpful empty state message is shown. The page has basic inline CSS for readability.

## Acceptance Criteria
- GET / calls ProductDirectory.listProducts() to get all product slugs
- For each slug, reads profile.json to extract identity.productName, metadata.url, metadata.scannedAt, completeness
- Renders an HTML page with a list/table of products
- Each product row shows: name (or slug as fallback), URL, scan date formatted as YYYY-MM-DD, completeness as N%
- Each product row links to /{slug}
- When listProducts() returns empty, shows 'No products scanned yet. Run basesignal scan <url> to get started.'
- When profile.json is missing or unreadable for a slug, the product still appears with fallback values
- HTML includes <title>Basesignal</title> and proper charset/viewport meta tags
- Basic inline CSS makes the list readable (clean font, table formatting, link styling)
- Tests verify: product list renders expected product names, links point to correct slugs, empty state is shown when no products exist
- npm run build succeeds
- All existing tests pass (npm run test:run)

## Files to Modify
- packages/cli/src/commands/view.ts

## Implementation Hints
- Extract renderPage(title, body) helper for HTML shell (reused by report page in E002)
- Extract escapeHtml() function — escape &, <, >, quote, apostrophe for XSS prevention
- Use ProductDirectory.readJson<ProductProfile>(slug, 'profile.json') per slug
- Type narrow identity/metadata fields: (profile?.identity as { productName?: string })?.productName
- For tests: create temp dir with ProductDirectory, write mock profile.json, start server, fetch GET /

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text: ## Implementation Plan ### Prerequisites This story depends on S001 (basesignal-m4m), which provides the view command registration, HTTP server scaffolding with `createServer`, route handling, and 404 page. This plan assumes S001 is complete and `packages/cli/src/commands/view.ts` exists with a working HTTP server that responds to GET / with a placeholder page. ### 1. Data Loading Strategy **ProductListItem interface** — define a simple struct for the product list: ```typescript interface ProductListItem { slug: string; name: string; url: string; scannedAt: string; // formatted as YYYY-MM-DD or "unknown" completeness: number; // 0-1 } ``` **loadProductList(productDir: ProductDirectory): ProductListItem[]** — iterate all slugs from `productDir.listProducts()` and read each profile: ```typescript const profile = productDir.readJson(slug, "profile.json"); ``` For each slug, extract: - `name`: `profile?.identity?.productName ?? slug` (slug as fallback) - `url`: `(profile?.metadata as { url?: string })?.url ?? ""` - `scannedAt`: `(profile?.metadata as { scannedAt?: number })?.scannedAt` — if truthy, format via `new Date(ts).toISOString().split("T")[0]`, else `"unknown"` - `completeness`: `(profile?.completeness as number) ?? 0` If `readJson` returns `null` (missing/corrupt profile.json), the product still appears with: `name=slug, url="", scannedAt="unknown", completeness=0`. ### 2. Type Narrowing Approach ProductProfile's `identity`, `metadata`, `revenue`, etc. are typed as optional interfaces or `Record`. The `identity` field is already a typed optional interface with `productName`. The `metadata` field is typed as `{ url: string; docsUrl?: string; scannedAt?: number } | undefined`. Use the same pattern as `packages/cli/src/formatters.ts`: - `profile?.identity?.productName` — direct access since `identity` is a typed optional interface - `(profile?.metadata as { url?: string; scannedAt?: number } | undefined)?.url` — type narrowing cast for metadata fields This is consistent with the existing codebase patterns in formatters.ts (lines 24, 42-46) and exportFormatters.ts (lines 58-69). ### 3. HTML Template Structure **escapeHtml(text: string): string** — a pure function that replaces the five HTML-significant characters: - `&` → `&` - `<` → `<` - `>` → `>` - `"` → `"` - `'` → `'` Use a single regex replace with a lookup map for efficiency. This is standard XSS prevention for user-derived content (product names, URLs). **renderPage(title: string, body: string): string** — returns a complete HTML document: ``` {title} {body} ``` Both `escapeHtml` and `renderPage` are exported so they can be reused by the product report page in E002. Keep them in view.ts for now — they can be extracted to a shared html-helpers.ts if they grow, but that's premature at this point. **renderProductList(products: ProductListItem[]): string** — builds the product list page: - Calls `renderPage("Basesignal", ...)` with either the product table or the empty state - The table has 4 columns: Product (name as link), URL, Scanned, Completeness - Product name links to `/{slug}` - All user-derived content (name, URL) goes through `escapeHtml()` - Completeness is rendered as `Math.round(completeness * 100)%` ### 4. CSS Approach Inline CSS within the `

title: Define value moment schema and types
description:
## Context

**Mission M003:** 7-Lens Value Discovery Validation
**Epic M003-E002:** Validation & Convergence - Clean and cluster lens output

**Story M003-E002-S001:** Define output structure for converged value moments.

## Specification

TypeScript types define the ValueMoment structure that represents a converged
value moment with tier, convergence count, contributing lenses, and merged
description. This is the output of the convergence pipeline.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] ValueMomentTier type includes: 1, 2, 3
2. [unit] ValueMoment type defines: id, name, tier, convergence_count, contributing_lenses[], description, roles[], product_surfaces[], contributing_candidates[]
3. [unit] ConvergenceResult type wraps array of ValueMoments with metadata
4. [unit] ValidatedCandidate type extends LensCandidate with validation_status
5. [unit] ValidationStatus type includes: valid, rewritten, removed
6. [unit] Schema exports are importable from convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts

## Guidance

Create convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts with types for validation status,
validated candidates, value moments, and convergence results.

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- The file `convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts` already exists with all required types
- Initial recommendation was to create a shared `lenses/types.ts` for `ValidatedCandidate extends LensCandidate`
- This was rejected by simplification review as unnecessary coupling

### Simplification Review
- Removed: Cross-module import from `lenses/types.ts` — false architectural coupling
- Removed: `extends LensCandidate` refactoring — `role` (singular) vs `roles` (plural) proves these are different domain concepts
- Kept: All existing types as standalone definitions — already minimal and correct

### Final Design
The existing `convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts` already satisfies all acceptance criteria. The implementation task is to verify the existing types match the spec and add unit tests confirming the type structure.

### Next Steps
- Write unit tests validating type exports and field shapes
- Verify existing consumers (clusterCandidates.ts) work with the types
- No code changes to the types file itself

---
