title: M005-E003-S001: Build ICPProfilesSection component
description:
## Story: M005-E003-S001 **Mission:** M005 - Product Profile View **Epic:** M005-E003 - ICP Profiles & Activation Map ## Outcome An ICPProfilesSection component displays 2-3 persona cards in a responsive grid. Each card shows name, description, confidence badge, value moment priorities, and collapsible subsections for triggers, pain points, and success metrics. ## Acceptance Criteria - [unit] Persona cards render in a responsive grid layout - [unit] Each card shows persona name, description, and confidence badge - [unit] Value moment priorities display with priority level and relevance reason - [unit] Collapsible subsections for activation triggers, pain points, success metrics - [unit] Empty state renders when no ICP profiles exist ## Implementation Plan ### Prerequisites - **Dependency:** basesignal-ohe (ProductProfilePage with tabs) must be completed first, or at minimum the `src/components/product-profile/` directory must exist - If the directory doesn't exist yet, create it as part of step 1 ### Step 1: Create `src/components/product-profile/ICPProfilesSection.tsx` Single flat file, ~80-100 lines. No sub-components extracted. **Imports:** - `useState` from react - `ChevronDown, ChevronRight` from lucide-react - `Collapsible, CollapsibleContent, CollapsibleTrigger` from `@/components/ui/collapsible` - `Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent` from `@/components/ui/card` - `Badge` from `@/components/ui/badge` - `ICPProfile` type from `convex/analysis/outputs/types` **Props interface:** ```typescript interface ICPProfilesSectionProps { profiles: ICPProfile[]; } ``` **Component structure:** 1. Empty state check: if `profiles.length === 0`, render dashed border div with "No ICP profiles generated yet." 2. Responsive grid: `grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4` 3. Per profile, render a Card containing: - **CardHeader**: flex row with CardTitle (profile.name) + confidence Badge - Badge className inline: `profile.confidence > 0.8 ? "bg-green-100 text-green-800" : profile.confidence > 0.6 ? "bg-amber-100 text-amber-800" : "bg-gray-100 text-gray-800"` - Badge text: `${Math.round(profile.confidence * 100)}%` with label "High"/"Medium"/"Low" - **CardDescription**: profile.description - **CardContent**: - Value moment priorities list: `
    ` with each priority showing `P{priority}` + relevance_reason - 3 Collapsible sections, each with own `useState(false)`: - "Activation Triggers" → profile.activation_triggers - "Pain Points" → profile.pain_points - "Success Metrics" → profile.success_metrics - Each collapsible: button trigger with chevron icon + section title, content is `
      ` of string items **Collapsible pattern** (match ActivityTimeline): ```tsx const [triggersOpen, setTriggersOpen] = useState(false); // ... same for painPointsOpen, metricsOpen
        {profile.activation_triggers.map((t, i) =>
      • {t}
        • )}
      ``` **Note:** Since there are 3 collapsible sections per card and multiple cards, the useState hooks must be per-card. Use a map/object state or render each card body as an inline section with 3 separate state variables using a key-based approach. Simplest: use the Collapsible component in uncontrolled mode (without open/onOpenChange) for the per-card sections, and use CSS to handle the chevron via data attributes. OR: since we're told to match the ActivityTimeline controlled pattern, render each card's collapsibles with their own state. To do this cleanly without extracting a component: use a single state object `Record` keyed by `${profile.id}-triggers`, `${profile.id}-painPoints`, `${profile.id}-metrics`. **Revised state approach:** ```typescript const [openSections, setOpenSections] = useState>({}); const toggle = (key: string) => setOpenSections(prev => ({ ...prev, [key]: !prev[key] })); ``` This keeps it flat (one useState) while handling multiple collapsibles across multiple cards. ### Step 2: Create `src/components/product-profile/ICPProfilesSection.test.tsx` **Test setup pattern** (match ActivityTimeline.test.tsx): ```typescript import { expect, test, describe } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"; import { ICPProfilesSection } from "./ICPProfilesSection"; import type { ICPProfile } from "convex/analysis/outputs/types"; ``` **Test helper — mock profile factory:** ```typescript function makeProfile(overrides?: Partial): ICPProfile { return { id: "icp-1", name: "Power User", description: "Highly engaged technical user", value_moment_priorities: [ { moment_id: "vm-1", priority: 1, relevance_reason: "Core workflow enabler" }, ], activation_triggers: ["Completes onboarding", "Creates first project"], pain_points: ["Slow data imports", "Limited API access"], success_metrics: ["Daily active usage", "API calls per week"], confidence: 0.85, sources: ["interview-1"], ...overrides, }; } ``` **Test cases (8 tests):** 1. **Empty state**: `profiles=[]` → shows "No ICP profiles generated yet." 2. **Renders persona name and description**: single profile → getByText(name), getByText(description) 3. **High confidence badge (>0.8)**: profile with confidence 0.85 → badge shows "85%" and has green classes 4. **Medium confidence badge (>0.6)**: profile with confidence 0.7 → badge shows "70%" and has amber classes 5. **Low confidence badge (<=0.6)**: profile with confidence 0.5 → badge shows "50%" and has gray classes 6. **Value moment priorities**: profile with 2 priorities → shows P1/P2 text + relevance reasons 7. **Collapsible sections toggle**: click "Activation Triggers" → content becomes visible; click "Pain Points" → content visible; click "Success Metrics" → content visible 8. **Multiple profiles render**: 2 profiles → both names visible in grid ### Step 3: Verify - Run `npm test -- --run ICPProfilesSection` to verify all tests pass - Ensure no lint errors with `npm run lint` ### Files to Create 1. `src/components/product-profile/ICPProfilesSection.tsx` (~80-100 lines) 2. `src/components/product-profile/ICPProfilesSection.test.tsx` (~120-150 lines) ### Files to Modify - None (unless product-profile/types.ts already exists from ValueMomentsSection story — if so, add ICPProfile re-export there) ### Edge Cases - profiles array is empty → empty state - confidence exactly 0.8 → should be "Medium" (>0.8 = High, not >=) - confidence exactly 0.6 → should be "Low" (>0.6 = Medium, not >=) - profile with empty activation_triggers/pain_points/success_metrics arrays → collapsible shows "(0)" count, empty list - Long persona descriptions → should wrap naturally in card ### Dependencies - basesignal-ohe (ProductProfilePage) blocks this — directory may not exist yet; create it if needed - Uses existing UI components: Card, Badge, Collapsible (all already in codebase) - Uses ICPProfile type from convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts (already exists) - lucide-react ChevronDown/ChevronRight (already used in ActivityTimeline) --- *Implementation plan via /plan-issue*

title: M007-E003-S001: Rewrite measurement spec prompt for Double Three-Layer Framework
description:
## Story: M007-E003-S001

**Mission:** M007 - Insight Quality
**Epic:** M007-E003 - Double Three-Layer Measurement Framework

## Outcome
The measurement spec system prompt follows the Double Three-Layer Framework: entities define reusable properties (designed once), events inherit all parent entity properties, activity names use past-tense verbs, one entity is the heartbeat, and the spec covers three perspectives (Customer, Product, Interaction).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Entity count reduced from 5-10 to 3-7, with heartbeat entity identification (isHeartbeat)
- Entity properties instruction: "Design 3-7 reusable properties per entity, inherited by ALL events"
- Events renamed to Activities with past-tense verbs (board_created, not board_create)
- Event property instruction: "Each event inherits ALL parent entity properties. Only specify ADDITIONAL event-specific properties."
- New perspective field per event: customer, product, or interaction
- New Step 4: Define User State Model (new, activated, active, at_risk, dormant) with event-based criteria
- Miro-style example included: Account, Board (heartbeat), Asset entities
- Anti-pattern: "Do NOT invent properties per event. Entity properties are inherited automatically."

## Files
- convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.ts (prompt at lines 16-97)

## Implementation Hints
Rewrite MEASUREMENT_SPEC_SYSTEM_PROMPT entirely following the Double Three-Layer Framework.
Reference: https://timo.space/blog/the-double-three-layer-framework-for-tracking-setups/
Keep output as JSON. Add isHeartbeat to entity schema, perspective to event schema, userStateModel as new top-level.
The prompt builder (lines 101-191) may need minor updates to format the new output instructions.

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-12-measurement-spec-prompt-design.md

---

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Rewrite the MEASUREMENT_SPEC_SYSTEM_PROMPT to follow the Double Three-Layer Framework with 4 steps: (1) Define Entities 3-7 with heartbeat + reusable properties, (2) Define Activities with past-tense verbs and property inheritance, (3) Assign Perspective per event, (4) Define User State Model with 5 states. Add minimal passthrough type changes (optional fields) and parser tolerance for new fields. Remove min-2 event property requirement.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `MEASUREMENT_SPEC_SYSTEM_PROMPT` in `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.ts` contains "Define Entities (3-7)" (not "5-10")
- [ ] Prompt contains "isHeartbeat" entity field instruction with "Mark exactly one as heartbeat"
- [ ] Prompt contains "3-7 reusable properties per entity, inherited by ALL events"
- [ ] Prompt contains past-tense verb instruction with examples "board_created, board_shared" (not "board_create")
- [ ] Prompt contains "Do NOT invent properties per event" anti-pattern instruction
- [ ] Prompt contains "perspective" field instruction with three values: customer, product, interaction
- [ ] Prompt contains "User State Model" step with 5 states: new, activated, active, at_risk, dormant
- [ ] Prompt contains Miro example with Account, Board (heartbeat), Asset entities
- [ ] JSON output schema in prompt includes `isHeartbeat` on entities, `perspective` on events, and `userStateModel` array
- [ ] `EntityDefinition` in `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts` has `isHeartbeat?: boolean`
- [ ] `TrackingEvent` in `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts` has `perspective?: string`
- [ ] `MeasurementSpec` in `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts` has `userStateModel?: unknown[]`
- [ ] Parser in `parseMeasurementSpecResponse` accepts entities with `isHeartbeat` field (passthrough, no strict validation)
- [ ] Parser accepts events with `perspective` field (passthrough, no strict validation)
- [ ] Parser accepts `userStateModel` array at top level (passthrough, no strict validation)
- [ ] Parser no longer rejects events with fewer than 2 properties (0 additional properties is valid)
- [ ] `npm test -- --run convex/analysis/outputs/` exits with 0 failures

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Add optional fields to types**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts`
   - Change: Add `isHeartbeat?: boolean` to `EntityDefinition` (line 69-74)
   - Change: Add `perspective?: string` to `TrackingEvent` (line 90-98)
   - Change: Add `userStateModel?: unknown[]` to `MeasurementSpec` (line 100-110)

2. **Rewrite MEASUREMENT_SPEC_SYSTEM_PROMPT**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.ts`
   - Change: Replace entire prompt constant (lines 16-97) with Double Three-Layer Framework prompt containing:
     - Step 1: Define Entities (3-7) with heartbeat identification and reusable properties
     - Step 2: Define Activities with past-tense verbs, property inheritance, anti-pattern
     - Step 3: Assign Perspective (customer/product/interaction) per event
     - Step 4: Define User State Model (5 states with event-based criteria)
     - Miro example (Account, Board heartbeat, Asset)
     - Updated JSON output schema with isHeartbeat, perspective, userStateModel
     - Rules section updated

3. **Update parser for new fields (passthrough)**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.ts`
   - Change in `parseEntities()` (line 205-286): After building entity object, copy `isHeartbeat` from raw if present (default false)
   - Change in event parsing loop (line 320-452): Copy `perspective` from raw if present
   - Change in event parsing loop: Remove min-2 property check (lines 360-364)
   - Change in `parseMeasurementSpecResponse()` return (line 480-491): Parse `userStateModel` from top-level if present, include in returned spec

4. **Update type compilation tests**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts`
   - Change: Add test verifying `EntityDefinition` accepts `isHeartbeat?: boolean`
   - Change: Add test verifying `TrackingEvent` accepts `perspective?: string`
   - Change: Add test verifying `MeasurementSpec` accepts `userStateModel?: unknown[]`

5. **Update prompt content tests**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.test.ts`
   - Change: Update "Define Entities (5-10)" test to check for "Define Entities (3-7)"
   - Change: Add prompt tests for: isHeartbeat, "inherited by ALL events", past-tense examples, "Do NOT invent properties per event", perspective, User State Model, Miro example
   - Change: Update "at least 2 properties" test to verify prompt no longer requires this
   - Change: Update "15-25" target check (unchanged in new prompt)

6. **Update parser tests**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.test.ts`
   - Change: Update `makeValidEntity` fixture to optionally include `isHeartbeat`
   - Change: Update `makeValidEvent` fixture to optionally include `perspective`
   - Change: Add test: parser accepts entities with `isHeartbeat: true`
   - Change: Add test: parser accepts events with `perspective: "product"`
   - Change: Add test: parser accepts top-level `userStateModel` array
   - Change: Add test: parser accepts events with 0 properties (remove min-2 test)
   - Change: Update fixture integration test to include new fields
   - Change: Remove "rejects events with fewer than 2 properties" test

### Test Plan
- [ ] `npm test -- --run convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts` passes with new optional field tests
- [ ] `npm test -- --run convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.test.ts` passes — all updated prompt content tests match new prompt, all parser tests pass with new passthrough fields, min-2 property rejection test removed
- [ ] `npm test -- --run` full suite passes (no regressions)

---
*Plan created via /plan-issue*

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:
## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Drop min-2 event property requirement — entity properties provide the base. Events with 0 additional properties are valid when they inherit everything from the entity.
- Use structured user state criteria `{ event_name, condition }[]` not free-text strings — enables programmatic validation in S002/S003.
- Keep parser entity bounds at 3-15 (current) — prompt instructs 3-7, parser flex is S002's concern.

### Product Perspective
- The Double Three-Layer Framework's core insight: design properties once at the entity level, inherit everywhere. This is the main prompt change.
- Three perspectives (customer/product/interaction) classify events by viewpoint, enabling coverage analysis in S003.
- User state model (5 states) connects to heartbeat entity — states should reference heartbeat activities.

### Simplification Review
- Reviewer flagged scope overlap between S001/S002/S003 — narrowed S001 to prompt rewrite + minimal passthrough parser changes
- Type definitions and strict parser validation deferred to S002 (where they're already scoped)
- Miro example enhanced with anti-pattern showing "DON'T repeat entity properties on events"
- Activities rename kept in prompt text only — TrackingEvent type rename deferred

### Final Design
S001 rewrites MEASUREMENT_SPEC_SYSTEM_PROMPT with 4 steps: (1) Define Entities 3-7 with heartbeat + reusable properties, (2) Define Activities with past-tense verbs and property inheritance, (3) Assign Perspective per event, (4) Define User State Model with 5 states. Parser gets minimal changes to accept new fields without strict validation. Types get optional fields for backward compat.

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover: (1) prompt text rewrite, (2) JSON output schema update, (3) minimal type additions (optional fields), (4) parser passthrough for new fields, (5) test fixture updates and new prompt content tests.

title: M010-E003-S001: Add lifecycle states progress phase and OutputsResult field
description:
## M010-E003-S001: Add Lifecycle States Progress Phase and OutputsResult Field

### Acceptance Criteria
- ProgressPhase union in types.ts includes 'outputs_lifecycle_states'
- OutputsResult in outputs/index.ts has lifecycle_states: LifecycleStatesResult | null
- PipelineOutputs in types.ts has lifecycle_states field
- npm run build in packages/mcp-server succeeds
- Existing tests pass — no regressions

### Proposed Solution
7 surgical insertions across 2 files, no logic changes:

File 1: `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/types.ts`
1. Add LifecycleStatesResult to @basesignal/core import
2. Add LifecycleStatesResult to re-export block
3. Add `| "outputs_lifecycle_states"` to ProgressPhase union (after "outputs_measurement_spec")
4. Add `lifecycle_states: LifecycleStatesResult | null;` to PipelineOutputs (after measurement_spec)

File 2: `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/index.ts`
5. Add LifecycleStatesResult to import from "../types.js"
6. Add `lifecycle_states: LifecycleStatesResult | null;` to OutputsResult
7. Add `lifecycle_states: null,` to result object in generateAllOutputs

Key decisions:
- Use `LifecycleStatesResult | null` (NOT `any`) — the `any` on activation_map is tech debt
- Import from ../types.js which re-exports core types (existing pattern)

### Design Doc
docs/plans/2026-02-22-lifecycle-states-progress-phase-design.md

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:

- Consolidate imports into existing import blocks

- No unnecessary abstractions — pure type additions only

Three surgical additions across two files (types.ts and outputs/index.ts):

title: M010-E001-S001: Define lifecycle state TypeScript types
description:
## M010-E001-S001: Define Lifecycle State TypeScript Types

### Acceptance Criteria
- StateCriterion interface has: event_name (string), condition (string), threshold (optional number)
- LifecycleState interface has: name, definition, entry_criteria (StateCriterion[]), exit_triggers (string[]), time_window (optional string)
- StateTransition interface has: from_state (string), to_state (string), trigger_conditions (string[]), typical_timeframe (optional string)
- LifecycleStatesResult interface has: states (LifecycleState[]), transitions (StateTransition[]), confidence (number), sources (string[])
- All four types exported from types/outputs.ts

### Proposed Solution
Add 4 interfaces to `packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts` in a new "Lifecycle States Types" section after the User State Model section (after line ~100), before Measurement Spec section. Use `// ---` comment-separator style.

Key decisions:
- StateCriterion is standalone (not extending UserStateCriterion) — parallel domain concepts
- exit_triggers uses string[] (narrative, human-readable), NOT StateCriterion[] — matches ActivationStage.trigger_events pattern
- trigger_conditions uses string[] for same reason
- LifecycleStatesResult follows confidence + sources pattern matching ActivationMap

### Files to Modify
- `packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts` — add 4 interfaces

### Design Doc
docs/plans/2026-02-22-lifecycle-types-design.md

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:
## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Lifecycle states are the canonical user progression model; downstream outputs derive from them
- Types should be clean and composable for Zod schemas, generator, and pipeline consumers

### Technical Perspective
- Two conceptual layers: structured StateCriterion[] on states, narrative string[] on transitions
- trigger_conditions as string[] follows existing StageTransition.trigger_events pattern
- Naming avoids collision: StateCriterion vs UserStateCriterion, LifecycleState vs UserState, StateTransition vs StageTransition

### Simplification Review
- Reviewer suggested cutting threshold and time_window — overridden by explicit acceptance criteria
- No other cuts; design is minimal

### Final Design
4 interfaces added to packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts after User State Model section: StateCriterion, LifecycleState, StateTransition, LifecycleStatesResult. Purely additive, follows existing patterns.

### Next Steps
Mechanical implementation: add the 4 interfaces with correct fields and optionality. Single file change.

**Design doc:** docs/plans/2026-02-22-lifecycle-types-design.md

---

title: M012-E001-S001: Entity Property and Activity Types
description:
Mission: M012 - Double Three-Layer Measurement Spec
Epic: E001 - Core Types and Zod Schemas
Story: S001 - Entity Property and Activity Types

## What
Define the building-block types for the Double Three-Layer Framework in @basesignal/core:
- EntityProperty (with rich type enum: string|number|boolean|array|id|calculated|experimental|temporary)
- ProductActivity (past-tense name, properties_supported[], activity_properties[])
- CustomerActivity (name, derivation_rule, properties_used[])
- InteractionActivity (name, properties_supported[])

## Acceptance Criteria
- EntityProperty type has: name, type (8-value enum), description, isRequired, variations? (optional string)
- EntityProperty Zod schema validates all 8 type enum values and rejects unknown values
- ProductActivity type has: name (string), properties_supported (string[]), activity_properties (EntityProperty[])
- CustomerActivity type has: name (string), derivation_rule (string), properties_used (string[])
- InteractionActivity type has: name (string), properties_supported (string[])
- All Zod schemas validate correctly
- Types in packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts, schemas in packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts
- Type tests and schema tests exist for each new type

## Files to Modify
- packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts
- packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts
- packages/core/src/types/__tests__/outputs.test.ts
- packages/core/src/schema/__tests__/outputs.test.ts

## Context
Add new types alongside existing ones (don't remove old types yet — S003 does that).
EntityProperty replaces EntityPropertyDef with richer type options from the book.
The variations field is for calculated properties (e.g., "1-30, 31-80, 81-150, 150+").
ProductActivity.activity_properties captures event-specific additions (e.g., share_method on 'shared').
CustomerActivity.derivation_rule example: "Board shared (first time) OR Asset created/updated (30+ times)"

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Add four new TypeScript interfaces and corresponding Zod schemas for the Double Three-Layer Framework building blocks: EntityProperty (with an 8-value type enum and optional variations field), ProductActivity, CustomerActivity, and InteractionActivity. These types are added alongside existing types (old types stay until S003). Tests cover both type-level assignability and runtime schema validation.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] \`EntityProperty\` interface in \`packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts\` has fields: name (string), type (EntityPropertyType — 8-value union), description (string), isRequired (boolean), variations (optional string)
- [ ] \`EntityPropertyType\` type alias in \`packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts\` equals \`"string" | "number" | "boolean" | "array" | "id" | "calculated" | "experimental" | "temporary"\`
- [ ] \`ProductActivity\` interface in \`packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts\` has fields: name (string), properties_supported (string[]), activity_properties (EntityProperty[])
- [ ] \`CustomerActivity\` interface in \`packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts\` has fields: name (string), derivation_rule (string), properties_used (string[])
- [ ] \`InteractionActivity\` interface in \`packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts\` has fields: name (string), properties_supported (string[])
- [ ] \`EntityPropertySchema\` in \`packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts\` validates all 8 type enum values and rejects unknown values (e.g., "object")
- [ ] \`ProductActivitySchema\` in \`packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts\` validates name (min 1), properties_supported (string[]), activity_properties (EntityProperty[])
- [ ] \`CustomerActivitySchema\` in \`packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts\` validates name (min 1), derivation_rule (min 1), properties_used (string[])
- [ ] \`InteractionActivitySchema\` in \`packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts\` validates name (min 1), properties_supported (string[])
- [ ] New schemas are exported from \`packages/core/src/schema/index.ts\`
- [ ] New types are exported from \`packages/core/src/index.ts\`
- [ ] Type tests in \`packages/core/src/types/__tests__/outputs.test.ts\` verify each new type's field assignability
- [ ] Schema tests in \`packages/core/src/schema/__tests__/outputs.test.ts\` verify acceptance of valid data and rejection of invalid data for each new schema
- [ ] \`npm test -- --run\` in \`packages/core\` exits with code 0

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Add EntityPropertyType and EntityProperty interface**
   - File: \`packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts\`
   - Add \`EntityPropertyType\` as a type alias for the 8-value union: \`"string" | "number" | "boolean" | "array" | "id" | "calculated" | "experimental" | "temporary"\`
   - Add \`EntityProperty\` interface with fields: name (string), type (EntityPropertyType), description (string), isRequired (boolean), variations? (string)
   - Place in a new section "Double Three-Layer Building Blocks" after the existing Entity/Property section

2. **Add ProductActivity, CustomerActivity, InteractionActivity interfaces**
   - File: \`packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts\`
   - Add \`ProductActivity\` with: name (string), properties_supported (string[]), activity_properties (EntityProperty[])
   - Add \`CustomerActivity\` with: name (string), derivation_rule (string), properties_used (string[])
   - Add \`InteractionActivity\` with: name (string), properties_supported (string[])
   - Place in the same new section, after EntityProperty

3. **Add EntityPropertyTypeSchema, EntityPropertySchema Zod schemas**
   - File: \`packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts\`
   - Add \`EntityPropertyTypeSchema = z.enum(["string", "number", "boolean", "array", "id", "calculated", "experimental", "temporary"])\`
   - Add \`EntityPropertySchema = z.object({ name: z.string().min(1), type: EntityPropertyTypeSchema, description: z.string().min(1), isRequired: z.boolean(), variations: z.string().optional() })\`
   - Place in a new section after the existing EntityPropertyDef/EntityDefinition schemas

4. **Add ProductActivitySchema, CustomerActivitySchema, InteractionActivitySchema**
   - File: \`packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts\`
   - \`ProductActivitySchema = z.object({ name: z.string().min(1), properties_supported: z.array(z.string()), activity_properties: z.array(EntityPropertySchema) })\`
   - \`CustomerActivitySchema = z.object({ name: z.string().min(1), derivation_rule: z.string().min(1), properties_used: z.array(z.string()) })\`
   - \`InteractionActivitySchema = z.object({ name: z.string().min(1), properties_supported: z.array(z.string()) })\`

5. **Export new schemas from schema barrel**
   - File: \`packages/core/src/schema/index.ts\`
   - Add \`EntityPropertyTypeSchema\`, \`EntityPropertySchema\`, \`ProductActivitySchema\`, \`CustomerActivitySchema\`, \`InteractionActivitySchema\` to the exports from \`"./outputs"\`
   - Add corresponding type exports

6. **Export new types from package barrel**
   - File: \`packages/core/src/index.ts\`
   - Add \`EntityPropertyType\`, \`EntityProperty\`, \`ProductActivity\`, \`CustomerActivity\`, \`InteractionActivity\` to the type exports from \`"./types/outputs"\`

7. **Add type tests for new types**
   - File: \`packages/core/src/types/__tests__/outputs.test.ts\`
   - Add \`describe("EntityProperty")\` — test that an object with all 8 type values is assignable, test optional variations field
   - Add \`describe("ProductActivity")\` — test all fields including nested EntityProperty[] in activity_properties
   - Add \`describe("CustomerActivity")\` — test all fields including derivation_rule
   - Add \`describe("InteractionActivity")\` — test all fields

8. **Add schema tests for new schemas**
   - File: \`packages/core/src/schema/__tests__/outputs.test.ts\`
   - Add \`describe("EntityPropertySchema")\`:
     - \`it.each\` for all 8 valid type values → accepts each
     - Rejects unknown type value (e.g., "object")
     - Accepts with optional variations present
     - Accepts with optional variations absent
     - Rejects empty name, empty description
   - Add \`describe("ProductActivitySchema")\`:
     - Accepts valid ProductActivity with nested activity_properties
     - Rejects empty name
     - Rejects missing properties_supported
   - Add \`describe("CustomerActivitySchema")\`:
     - Accepts valid CustomerActivity
     - Rejects empty name, empty derivation_rule
     - Rejects missing properties_used
   - Add \`describe("InteractionActivitySchema")\`:
     - Accepts valid InteractionActivity
     - Rejects empty name
     - Rejects missing properties_supported

### Test Plan
- [ ] EntityProperty type test: create objects with each of the 8 type values — all compile and pass
- [ ] EntityProperty schema test: \`EntityPropertySchema.safeParse\` accepts all 8 types via \`it.each\`, rejects "object", accepts/omits variations
- [ ] ProductActivity type test: create object with nested EntityProperty[] — compiles and passes
- [ ] ProductActivity schema test: \`ProductActivitySchema.safeParse\` accepts valid, rejects empty name
- [ ] CustomerActivity type test: create object with derivation_rule — compiles and passes
- [ ] CustomerActivity schema test: \`CustomerActivitySchema.safeParse\` accepts valid, rejects empty derivation_rule
- [ ] InteractionActivity type test: create object with properties_supported — compiles and passes
- [ ] InteractionActivity schema test: \`InteractionActivitySchema.safeParse\` accepts valid, rejects empty name
- [ ] Run \`npm test -- --run\` in packages/core — exits with code 0, all existing + new tests pass

---

title: Add validation to measurement spec LLM response parser
M010-E002-S002: Response parser for lifecycle states
description:
## M010-E002-S002: Response Parser for Lifecycle States

### Acceptance Criteria
- parseLifecycleStatesResponse extracts JSON from LLM response using extractJson
- Validates extracted JSON against LifecycleStatesResultSchema
- Valid 7-state response parses successfully
- Missing required fields throws descriptive error
- Invalid state structure (e.g. missing entry_criteria) throws descriptive error

### Proposed Solution
Add to `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/lifecycle-states.ts` (file created by S001):

```typescript
import { extractJson, LifecycleStatesResultSchema } from "@basesignal/core";
import type { LifecycleStatesResult } from "@basesignal/core";

export function parseLifecycleStatesResponse(text: string): LifecycleStatesResult {
  const raw = extractJson(text);
  return LifecycleStatesResultSchema.parse(raw);
}
```

Key decisions:
- 3-line Zod-first pattern — establishes new standard replacing 50-100 line hand-written parsers
- Let ZodError propagate unmodified — already includes field paths and type expectations
- No custom error wrapping needed — errors surface as PipelineError objects

### Design Doc
docs/plans/2026-02-22-lifecycle-states-response-parser-design.md

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Add a `parseLifecycleStatesResponse` function to the lifecycle-states output module that extracts JSON from LLM text responses and validates it against the Zod schema from `@basesignal/core`. This is a 3-line function that establishes the Zod-first parser pattern, replacing the legacy hand-written validation approach used by existing parsers (activation-map, icp-profiles, measurement-spec).

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/lifecycle-states.ts` exports `parseLifecycleStatesResponse(text: string): LifecycleStatesResult`
- [ ] Function calls `extractJson(text)` from `@basesignal/core` to extract JSON from markdown-fenced or raw LLM responses
- [ ] Function calls `LifecycleStatesResultSchema.parse(raw)` from `@basesignal/core` to validate and return typed result
- [ ] Calling with valid 7-state JSON string returns a typed `LifecycleStatesResult` object
- [ ] Calling with JSON missing required fields throws `ZodError` with field path info (e.g. `states[0].name`)
- [ ] Calling with invalid nested structure (e.g. missing `entry_criteria`) throws `ZodError` with nested path info

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Add parser imports to lifecycle-states.ts**
   - File: `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/lifecycle-states.ts`
   - Change: Add imports for `extractJson`, `LifecycleStatesResultSchema`, and `LifecycleStatesResult` from `@basesignal/core`
   - Note: This file is created by dependency S001 (basesignal-yts). If it doesn't exist yet, create it with just the parser function.

2. **Add parseLifecycleStatesResponse function**
   - File: `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/lifecycle-states.ts`
   - Change: Add exported function that calls `extractJson(text)` then `LifecycleStatesResultSchema.parse(raw)` and returns the result
   - Pattern: 3 lines — extract, validate, return. No try/catch wrapping.

### Test Plan
- [ ] Verify function exists: `import { parseLifecycleStatesResponse } from './lifecycle-states.js'` compiles without error
- [ ] Verify valid parse: Call with `JSON.stringify(valid7StateObject)` → returns typed `LifecycleStatesResult` with 7 states
- [ ] Verify markdown fence handling: Call with `` ```json\n{...}\n``` `` → parses correctly (via extractJson)
- [ ] Verify missing field error: Call with JSON missing `states` → throws ZodError mentioning `states`
- [ ] Verify nested error: Call with state missing `entry_criteria` → throws ZodError with path like `states[0].entry_criteria`
- [ ] Verify `npm test` passes in `packages/mcp-server`

---
*Plan created via /plan-issue*

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:
## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Use Zod-only validation (LifecycleStatesResultSchema.parse) — existing hand-written parsers are legacy, this establishes the new pattern
- Let ZodError propagate unmodified — it already includes field paths, type expectations, and received values
- Return type derived from schema via LifecycleStatesResult from @basesignal/core

### Simplification Review
- Design is irreducible: 3 lines (extract, validate, return)
- No wrapping, no custom error messages, no boilerplate
- Verdict: APPROVED — nothing to remove or simplify

### Final Design
```typescript
export function parseLifecycleStatesResponse(text: string): LifecycleStatesResult {
  const raw = extractJson(text);
  return LifecycleStatesResultSchema.parse(raw);
}
```

Two error paths: SyntaxError (bad JSON from extractJson) and ZodError (invalid structure from schema.parse). Both satisfy 'throws descriptive error' acceptance criteria.

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: imports, function placement in lifecycle-states.ts, export from module

title: [M013-E001-S001] View Command and HTTP Server
description:
## Story: M013-E001-S001 — View Command and HTTP Server
**Mission:** M013 — View Command — Browser-Based Profile Viewing
**Epic:** M013-E001 — View Command & HTTP Server

## Outcome
A new `basesignal view [slug]` command is registered in the CLI. When run, it starts a local HTTP server on port 3700 (overridable with --port), prints the URL to stderr, and shuts down cleanly on Ctrl+C / SIGINT. The server responds to GET / with a placeholder HTML page. Unknown routes return a 404 page. The command is wired into program.ts following the existing registration pattern.

## Acceptance Criteria
- packages/cli/src/commands/view.ts exists with a registerViewCommand function
- registerViewCommand is called in program.ts alongside the other register* calls
- The command accepts an optional [slug] positional argument
- The command has a --port <number> option defaulting to 3700
- Running the command starts an HTTP server using Node built-in http module
- Server listens on localhost at the configured port
- Server prints 'Listening on http://localhost:<port>' to stderr
- GET / responds with 200 and an HTML page (placeholder content is fine)
- GET /unknown responds with 404 and an HTML error page
- Ctrl+C / SIGINT closes the server and exits the process cleanly
- Tests verify: server starts on a random port, GET / returns 200 HTML, GET /unknown returns 404, server.close() works
- npm run build succeeds across all packages
- All existing tests pass (npm run test:run)

## Files to Modify
- packages/cli/src/commands/view.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/program.ts

## Implementation Hints
- Follow export.ts registration pattern: registerViewCommand(program)
- Use Node built-in http.createServer — no Express/Fastify dependency
- View command creates its own ProductDirectory from config.storagePath + '/products'
- Use port 0 in tests for random available port
- Clean shutdown: process.on('SIGINT', () => server.close(() => process.exit(0)))

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:
## Implementation Plan ### 1. New File: `packages/cli/src/commands/view.ts` #### Exported Functions **`startViewServer(options: ViewServerOptions): Promise`** - Testable core that creates and starts the HTTP server - Signature: ```ts interface ViewServerOptions { port: number; storagePath: string; } interface ViewServerHandle { url: string; // e.g. "http://localhost:3700" port: number; // actual port (important when port 0 is used) close: () => Promise; } ``` - Creates `http.createServer(requestHandler)` - Calls `server.listen(port, '127.0.0.1')` and wraps in a Promise that resolves on the `listening` event - Returns a handle with the actual port from `server.address()`, a formatted URL, and a `close()` method that wraps `server.close()` in a Promise - This separation from the command action allows tests to start/stop the server without touching Commander or process signals **`requestHandler(req: IncomingMessage, res: ServerResponse): void`** - Internal (not exported, but testable via the server) - Route logic: - `GET /` → 200, `Content-Type: text/html`, placeholder HTML body: `Basesignal

Basesignal

Profile viewer coming soon.

` - Everything else → 404, `Content-Type: text/html`, error HTML: `Not Found

404 — Not Found

` **`registerViewCommand(program: Command): void`** - Follows the `registerInitCommand` pattern (takes only `program`, no storage/provider factories — the view command constructs its own `ProductDirectory` internally when it needs it in future stories) - Registers: `program.command("view [slug]")` - `.description("View a product profile in the browser")` - `.option("-p, --port ", "HTTP server port", "3700")` - `.action(async (slug, opts) => { ... })` - Action body: 1. Parse port: `const port = Number(opts.port)` — validate it's a finite positive integer, error and exit if not 2. Call `const handle = await startViewServer({ port, storagePath: loadConfig().storagePath })` 3. `console.error(\`Listening on ${handle.url}\`)` 4. Register SIGINT handler: `process.on('SIGINT', () => { handle.close().then(() => process.exit(0)); })` 5. (The function never returns — the server keeps the event loop alive until SIGINT) ### 2. Modified File: `packages/cli/src/program.ts` - Add import: `import { registerViewCommand } from "./commands/view.js";` - Add registration call after `registerServeCommand`: `registerViewCommand(program);` - The view command takes only `program` (like `registerInitCommand`) — no storage/provider factories needed since it will construct its own `ProductDirectory` from `loadConfig().storagePath` internally ### 3. Test File: `packages/cli/src/commands/view.test.ts` Uses Vitest, follows the patterns from `serve.test.ts` and `export.test.ts`. #### Test Cases **"view command registration"** - `registerViewCommand` registers a "view" command on the program - The command has description containing "browser" or "view" - The command accepts optional `[slug]` argument - The command has a `--port` option **"HTTP server lifecycle"** - `startViewServer({ port: 0, storagePath: '/tmp/test' })` starts successfully and returns a handle with a non-zero port - `handle.close()` resolves and stops the server (subsequent fetch to handle.url rejects) **"GET / returns 200 with HTML"** - Start server with port 0 - `const res = await fetch(handle.url + "/")` - `expect(res.status).toBe(200)` - `expect(res.headers.get("content-type")).toContain("text/html")` - Body contains `Basesignal` - Close server in cleanup **"GET /unknown returns 404 with HTML"** - Start server with port 0 - `const res = await fetch(handle.url + "/unknown")` - `expect(res.status).toBe(404)` - `expect(res.headers.get("content-type")).toContain("text/html")` - Body contains "Not Found" - Close server in cleanup **"server listens on 127.0.0.1 (localhost)"** - Start server with port 0 - `handle.url` starts with `http://localhost:` or `http://127.0.0.1:` #### Test Approach - Use `port: 0` everywhere so OS picks a random available port — no port conflicts - Use native `fetch` (available in Node 18+) — no extra test deps - Each test starts its own server and closes it in an `afterEach` or try/finally block - No mocking needed for the HTTP server tests — they are real integration tests against localhost - For command registration tests, use Commander directly like `serve.test.ts` ### 4. Edge Cases - **Invalid port:** Non-numeric or out-of-range `--port` value → print error to stderr, `process.exit(1)` - **Port already in use:** `server.listen` emits `error` event with `EADDRINUSE` → catch and print a helpful error: "Port {N} is already in use. Try a different port with --port." - **SIGINT during startup:** The `process.on('SIGINT')` handler is registered only after `server.listen` resolves, but Node will default-exit on SIGINT before that, which is acceptable - **slug argument:** Accepted but unused in this story (it's for S002). Just store it for later use; the placeholder page is shown regardless ### 5. No New Dependencies - Uses only `node:http` (built-in) — no Express, Fastify, or other HTTP framework - `commander` already in deps for option/command registration - Tests use native `fetch` (Node 18+)

title: Orchestrate all output generators into single pipeline
description:
## Context

**Mission M004:** Actionable Output Generation
**Epic M004-E004:** Measurement Spec Generation

**Story M004-E004-S004:** Orchestrate all output generators into single pipeline

**Dependencies:** Requires M004-E004-S003 (measurement spec test action)

## Specification

An orchestration action runs all output generators in sequence (ICP → Map → Spec) and returns a complete OutputGenerationResult with all three outputs.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] generateAllOutputs internalAction accepts productId and returns OutputGenerationResult
2. [unit] Runs ICP generation first
3. [unit] Runs activation map generation second
4. [unit] Runs measurement spec generation third (using ICP and map)
5. [integration] Result includes all three outputs: icp_profiles, activation_map, measurement_spec
6. [integration] testGenerateAllOutputs public action available for dashboard testing
7. [integration] Linear test completes in under 60 seconds
8. [integration] Errors in one generator don't block others (partial results returned)

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/lenses/orchestrate.ts
- convex/analysis/orchestrate.ts

---
*Product Story: M004-E004-S004 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Strict sequential execution (not parallel) — ACs explicitly number the order and sequential is simpler to reason about
- Error isolation via try/catch per step — matches existing pattern from lenses orchestrator
- Follow established orchestration patterns from convex/analysis/lenses/orchestrate.ts

### Simplification Review
- Reviewer suggested removing testGenerateAllOutputs but AC #6 requires it — kept as thin passthrough
- No retry logic, no DB storage in orchestrator, no progress tracking — correctly excluded per YAGNI

### Final Design
- File: convex/analysis/outputs/orchestrate.ts
- Sequential pipeline: ICP → Map → Spec, each in try/catch
- OutputGenerationResult with nullable fields for partial results
- GENERATION_STEPS exported const for testing step order
- generateAllOutputs (internalAction) + testGenerateAllOutputs (public action)
- Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-08-output-orchestration-pipeline-design.md

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: file creation, type extension for nullable fields, sequential handler, test action, unit tests

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

## Implementation Plan

### Summary

Create `convex/analysis/outputs/orchestrate.ts` with a sequential pipeline that runs all three M004 output generators (ICP profiles → Activation map → Measurement spec) in order, isolating errors per step so partial results are returned. Export `GENERATION_STEPS` constant for test verification, `generateAllOutputs` internalAction for programmatic use, and `testGenerateAllOutputs` public action for dashboard testing.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `convex/analysis/outputs/orchestrate.ts` exports `generateAllOutputs` as an `internalAction` with `args: { productId: v.id("products") }` and return type `OutputGenerationResult`
- [ ] AC2: `GENERATION_STEPS` exported const equals `["icp", "activation_map", "measurement_spec"]` — first element is `"icp"`, verified by unit test
- [ ] AC3: `GENERATION_STEPS[1]` equals `"activation_map"` — verified by unit test
- [ ] AC4: `GENERATION_STEPS[2]` equals `"measurement_spec"` — verified by unit test
- [ ] AC5: `OutputGenerationResult` interface in `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts` has fields `icp_profiles: ICPProfile[]`, `activation_map: ActivationMap | null`, `measurement_spec: MeasurementSpec | null`
- [ ] AC6: `convex/analysis/outputs/orchestrate.ts` exports `testGenerateAllOutputs` as a public `action` that delegates to `generateAllOutputs` via `ctx.runAction()`
- [ ] AC7: Integration test via dashboard — `testGenerateAllOutputs` completes in under 60 seconds (manual verification)
- [ ] AC8: Each generator call is wrapped in its own `try/catch` — if one fails, subsequent generators still run and result includes `errors` array with `{ step, error }` entries

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Add `OutputGenerationResult` to types file**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts`
   - Add the `OutputGenerationResult` interface with nullable fields for partial results:
     ```typescript
     export interface OutputGenerationResult {
       productId: string;
       icp_profiles: ICPProfile[];
       activation_map: ActivationMap | null;
       measurement_spec: MeasurementSpec | null;
       errors?: Array<{ step: string; error: string }>;
       generated_at: string;
       execution_time_ms: number;
     }
     ```
   - Note: The types file must already exist from M004-E001-S001. If it doesn't exist yet, create it with this type and minimal imports.

2. **Create the orchestration module**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/orchestrate.ts`
   - Export `GENERATION_STEPS = ["icp", "activation_map", "measurement_spec"] as const`
   - Export `GenerationStep` type derived from the const array
   - Import `internalAction`, `action`, `internal`, `v` from Convex
   - Import `OutputGenerationResult`, `ICPProfile`, `ActivationMap`, `MeasurementSpec` from `./types`

3. **Implement `generateAllOutputs` internalAction**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/orchestrate.ts`
   - Args: `{ productId: v.id("products") }`
   - Handler logic:
     - Record `startTime = Date.now()`
     - Initialize `errors: Array<{ step: string; error: string }> = []`
     - Step 1: `try { icp_profiles = await ctx.runAction(internal.analysis.outputs.generateICPProfiles.generateICPProfiles, { productId }) } catch (e) { errors.push({ step: "icp", error: String(e) }); icp_profiles = [] }`
     - Step 2: `try { activation_map = await ctx.runAction(internal.analysis.outputs.generateActivationMap.generateActivationMap, { productId }) } catch (e) { errors.push({ step: "activation_map", error: String(e) }); activation_map = null }`
     - Step 3: `try { measurement_spec = await ctx.runAction(internal.analysis.outputs.generateMeasurementSpec.generateMeasurementSpec, { productId }) } catch (e) { errors.push({ step: "measurement_spec", error: String(e) }); measurement_spec = null }`
     - Return `OutputGenerationResult` with all fields, `errors` only if non-empty, `generated_at: new Date().toISOString()`, `execution_time_ms: Date.now() - startTime`

4. **Implement `testGenerateAllOutputs` public action**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/orchestrate.ts`
   - Thin passthrough following `testRunAllLenses` pattern from `convex/analysis/lenses/orchestrate.ts`
   - Args: `{ productId: v.id("products") }`
   - Delegates to `internal.analysis.outputs.orchestrate.generateAllOutputs` via `ctx.runAction()`
   - Single `console.log` with summary (ICP count, map stages, spec events, time, errors)
   - Returns the full result

5. **Write unit tests**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/orchestrate.test.ts`
   - Test `GENERATION_STEPS` constant:
     - Has exactly 3 elements
     - Elements are `["icp", "activation_map", "measurement_spec"]` in that order
     - First is `"icp"` (AC2), second is `"activation_map"` (AC3), third is `"measurement_spec"` (AC4)
   - Test `OutputGenerationResult` type shape (following `AllLensesResult` test pattern from `convex/analysis/lenses/orchestrate.test.ts`):
     - Can be constructed with all fields populated (success case)
     - Can be constructed with nullable fields as null and errors array (partial failure case)
     - `errors` field is optional (omitted when no errors)

### Test Plan

- [ ] **Unit: GENERATION_STEPS order** — `convex/analysis/outputs/orchestrate.test.ts` verifies `GENERATION_STEPS` equals `["icp", "activation_map", "measurement_spec"]` and has length 3 (covers AC2, AC3, AC4)
- [ ] **Unit: OutputGenerationResult type** — `convex/analysis/outputs/orchestrate.test.ts` verifies the type can be constructed with all-success, partial-failure, and all-failure scenarios (covers AC1, AC5, AC8 shape)
- [ ] **Verify `npm test` passes** — Run `npm run test:run` and confirm 0 failures including the new test file
- [ ] **Integration: dashboard test** — Manual: call `testGenerateAllOutputs` from Convex dashboard with a product that has value moments and activation levels; verify result contains all three output fields and completes in under 60s (covers AC5, AC6, AC7)

---
*Plan created via /plan-issue*

title: Update formatProfile.ts to use new MeasurementSpec perspectives structure
description:
## Problem
formatProfile.ts (lines 128-133) accesses old `profile.entities.items` and `profile.entities.confidence` structure. This is the legacy entity model output, not MeasurementSpec, but should be updated to render the new perspective-grouped entities from MeasurementSpec instead.

## Context
Discovered during retro after M012 Double Three-Layer MeasurementSpec implementation. The formatter still renders the old flat entity list instead of the richer perspective-grouped structure.

## Solution
Replace the entities section with a measurement_spec section that renders product/customer/interaction perspectives, their entities, and activities.

title: M007-E003-S002: Update types and validation for property inheritance and heartbeat
description:
## Story: M007-E003-S002

**Mission:** M007 - Insight Quality
**Epic:** M007-E003 - Double Three-Layer Measurement Framework

## Outcome
TypeScript types and parser validation enforce the Double Three-Layer Framework: EntityDefinition has isHeartbeat, TrackingEvent has perspective, event properties are validated as event-specific additions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- EntityDefinition gains isHeartbeat: boolean
- TrackingEvent gains perspective: "customer" | "product" | "interaction"
- New UserState interface: { name, definition, criteria: { event_name, condition }[] }
- MeasurementSpec gains userStateModel: UserState[]
- Parser validates exactly one heartbeat entity
- Parser validates valid perspective field on each event
- Parser warns if event properties duplicate parent entity property names
- Parser validates userStateModel has 5 states (new, activated, active, at_risk, dormant)
- All existing measurement spec tests updated and passing

## Files
- convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts
- convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.ts (parser)
- convex/analysis/outputs/types.test.ts
- convex/analysis/outputs/generateMeasurementSpec.test.ts

## Depends On
- M007-E003-S001 (prompt rewrite)

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Warnings belong as `warnings?: string[]` on MeasurementSpec — testable, composable for S003
- isHeartbeat defaults to false via `=== true` check for backward compatibility
- Property duplication is a warning (non-blocking), all other validations are errors (throw)
- parseUserStateModel() validates exact 5 states with required names

### Simplification Review
- Reviewer flagged bundling of multiple concepts — but all are explicit ACs, so kept
- No new files or abstractions — everything stays in existing types.ts and generateMeasurementSpec.ts
- Parser validation kept structural and mechanical

### Final Design
Add isHeartbeat to EntityDefinition, perspective to TrackingEvent, UserState/UserStateCriterion interfaces, userStateModel and warnings to MeasurementSpec. Parser gains heartbeat count validation, perspective validation, property duplication warnings, and parseUserStateModel() function. All existing test fixtures updated with new required fields.

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover: (1) type changes, (2) parser changes with validation order, (3) test fixture updates, (4) new test cases

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-12-measurement-types-validation-design.md

title: M012-E002-S001: System Prompt Rewrite
M010-E003-S002: Wire lifecycle states generator into pipeline
description:
## M010-E003-S002: Wire Lifecycle States Generator into Pipeline

### Acceptance Criteria
- generateAllOutputs imports and calls generateLifecycleStates
- Runs after activation map (requires activation map result as input)
- Progress callback fires: started before, completed after success, failed on error
- Errors pushed to errors array
- Skipped if activationLevels is null
- Generator receives identity, value moments, activation levels, activation map

### Proposed Solution
3 changes to `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/index.ts`:

1. Import + re-export generateLifecycleStates from "./lifecycle-states.js"
2. Insert step 3 between activation map and measurement spec:
   Guard: `if (activationLevels && result.activation_map && identity)` — includes identity because generator needs IdentityResult (not nullable)
   try/catch with progress callbacks and error push
   Input: { identity, value_moments, activation_levels, activation_map }
3. Renumber measurement spec comment from // 3. to // 4.

Key decisions:
- Guard includes `identity` — type narrowing from IdentityResult | null to IdentityResult
- Story hints say `if (activationLevels && result.activation_map)` but design adds identity guard to avoid type error
- Follows exact try/catch + progress callback pattern of existing generators

### Design Doc
docs/plans/2026-02-22-wire-lifecycle-states-pipeline-design.md

title: M005-E002-S001: Create ProductProfilePage with tabs
description:
## Story: M005-E002-S001
**Mission:** M005 - Product Profile View
**Epic:** M005-E002 - Profile Page Shell & Value Moments

## Outcome
A tabbed profile page at /products/:productId shows the product header (name, URL, completeness, confidence) and four tabs for Value Moments, ICP Profiles, Activation Map, and Measurement Spec. Loading and empty states are handled.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [unit] ProductProfilePage reads productId from useParams and fetches product + profile data
- [unit] Header displays product name, URL, completeness %, and confidence score
- [unit] Back link navigates to /
- [unit] Four tabs render: Value Moments, ICP Profiles, Activation Map, Measurement Spec
- [unit] Loading state renders while data is being fetched
- [unit] Empty state renders when profile has no data

## Implementation Hints
Create src/routes/ProductProfilePage.tsx. useParams for productId, useQuery for product (api.products.get) and profile (api.productProfiles.get). Header with summary stats. Tabs component with 4 tab triggers and content areas. Each tab content is placeholder until section components are built.

## Reference Files
- src/components/ui/tabs.tsx
- src/components/ui/card.tsx
- convex/products.ts
- convex/productProfiles.ts

---
*Product Story: M005-E002-S001 · Created via /product-story-handoff*
notes:
## Implementation Plan

### Prerequisites
- **Dependency:** basesignal-hkn (ProductsListPage + routing) must complete first since it adds the `/products/:productId` route to App.tsx
- If implementing before that story, add the route to App.tsx ourselves (Step 3 below)

### Step 1: Create `src/routes/ProductProfilePage.tsx`

Single flat component following EntityDetailPage pattern. ~80 lines.

**Imports:**
- `useParams`, `Link` from react-router-dom
- `useQuery` from convex/react
- `api` from `../../convex/_generated/api`
- `Id` type from `../../convex/_generated/dataModel`
- `Badge` from `@/components/ui/badge`
- `Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent` from `@/components/ui/tabs`
- `Card, CardContent` from `@/components/ui/card`
- `ArrowLeft` from lucide-react

**Component structure:**

```tsx
export default function ProductProfilePage() {
  const { productId } = useParams<{ productId: string }>()

  const product = useQuery(
    api.products.get,
    productId ? { id: productId as Id<"products"> } : "skip"
  )
  const profile = useQuery(
    api.productProfiles.get,
    productId ? { productId: productId as Id<"products"> } : "skip"
  )

  // Loading state (product === undefined)
  if (product === undefined) {
    return loading spinner
  }

  // Not found state (product === null)  
  if (!product) {
    return not found + back link
  }

  const completeness = profile?.completeness ?? 0
  const confidence = profile?.overallConfidence ?? 0

  return header + tabs with placeholders
}
```

**Key decisions:**
- Use "skip" pattern for conditional queries (same as JourneyEditorPage)
- Profile can be null when product exists but no profile created yet — show 0% stats, not an error
- Tabs placeholders use Card with centered text — easy to swap for section components later
- Back link uses ArrowLeft icon consistent with other navigation

### Step 2: Create `src/routes/ProductProfilePage.test.tsx`

Mock convex/react useQuery and react-router-dom useParams.
Wrap in MemoryRouter for Link rendering.

**6 test cases (1:1 with acceptance criteria):**

1. **Reads productId and fetches data**: useParams returns productId, verify useQuery called with correct args
2. **Header shows name, URL, completeness %, confidence %**: Mock product + profile → verify text renders
3. **Back link navigates to /**: verify link with href "/"
4. **Four tabs render**: getByRole("tab") for Value Moments, ICP Profiles, Activation Map, Measurement Spec
5. **Loading state**: useQuery returns undefined → "Loading product..."
6. **Empty state when profile null**: product loaded, profile null → shows 0% stats

### Step 3: Add route to App.tsx (if not done by basesignal-hkn)

```tsx
<Route path="products/:productId" element={<ProductProfilePage />} />
```

### Step 4: Verify

- `npm test -- --run ProductProfilePage` — all 6 tests pass
- `npm run lint` — no errors

### Files to Create
1. `src/routes/ProductProfilePage.tsx` (~80 lines)
2. `src/routes/ProductProfilePage.test.tsx` (~100 lines)

### Files to Modify
1. `src/App.tsx` — add route + import (only if basesignal-hkn not done first)

### Edge Cases
- productId missing from URL params → queries use "skip", shows loading
- Product exists but no profile → completeness/confidence default to 0%
- Profile still loading while product loaded → show page with 0% stats

### Dependencies
- Uses existing: api.products.get, api.productProfiles.get, Tabs, Card, Badge, ArrowLeft
- Blocked by basesignal-hkn for routing, but component can be built independently

---
*Implementation plan via /plan-issue*

comments.text:
## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Flat component (Option A) following EntityDetailPage pattern — no wrappers or premature abstractions
- useParams + two useQuery calls with skip pattern for data fetching
- Three states: loading (undefined), not found (null), loaded (truthy)
- Tabs with placeholder Cards ready for section component swap in later stories

### Simplification Review
- Nothing to remove, nothing to simplify
- Design is inevitable: proven patterns + existing data model
- Verdict: APPROVED

### Final Design
Single flat ProductProfilePage.tsx with header (name, URL, completeness %, confidence %), four tabs (Value Moments, ICP Profiles, Activation Map, Measurement Spec), loading/empty/not-found states. 6 unit tests mapping 1:1 to acceptance criteria.

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover:
1. Create ProductProfilePage.tsx following EntityDetailPage pattern
2. Add route to App.tsx
3. Write 6 unit tests

title: Remove deprecated EntityPropertyDef and EntityDefinition types
description:
## Problem
`EntityPropertyDef` and `EntityDefinition` in core/src/types/outputs.ts (lines 69-82) are marked `@deprecated` but still exported. No production code imports them — only internal test fixtures and Zod schemas reference them. They can be removed to reduce API surface confusion.

## Context
Discovered during retro after M012 Double Three-Layer MeasurementSpec implementation. These types were part of the old flat entity model, replaced by ProductEntity, CustomerEntity, InteractionEntity and their property/activity types.

## Solution
Remove the deprecated types, their Zod schemas, their exports from index.ts and schema/index.ts, and their test fixtures. Keep the new Double Three-Layer types only.

title: Implement LLM-powered ICP profile generator
description:
## Context

**Mission M004:** Actionable Output Generation
**Epic M004-E002:** ICP Profile Generation

**Story M004-E002-S002:** Implement LLM-powered ICP profile generator

**Dependencies:** Requires M004-E002-S001 (ICP data aggregation)

## Specification

An internalAction uses Claude to generate 2-3 distinct ICP profiles from aggregated role and value moment data, producing personas with prioritized value moments, activation triggers, and success metrics.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] generateICPProfiles internalAction accepts productId and returns ICPProfile[]
2. [unit] Prompt includes aggregated role data and value moments
3. [unit] LLM output is parsed into ICPProfile[] structure
4. [unit] Produces exactly 2-3 personas (enforced by prompt)
5. [integration] Each persona has distinct value_moment_priorities (not identical lists)
6. [integration] Persona names are specific, not generic (contains role context)
7. [integration] Linear test produces PM, Engineer, and Team Lead personas

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts
- convex/analysis/lenses/shared.ts

---
*Product Story: M004-E002-S002 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Follow Option A (inline fetching in internalAction) matching the existing lens extractor pattern
- Unit tests cover pure functions (prompt builder, parser); internalAction orchestrates the full flow
- Inline aggregation logic since S001 hasn't landed yet; add TODO for future extraction
- Parser enforces 2-3 count constraint AND distinctness validation structurally

### Simplification Review
- Removed standalone validateDistinctPriorities helper — folded into parseICPProfiles
- Removed separate ICPInputData interface — build prompt directly from raw data
- Combined all validation into single pass in parser
- Added TODO comment requirement about S001 dependency

### Final Design
Two pure functions + one constant + one internalAction:
- `ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT` constant with persona generation instructions
- `buildICPPrompt(roles, valueMoments, targetCustomer)` pure function
- `parseICPProfiles(responseText)` pure function with count + field + distinctness validation
- `generateICPProfiles` internalAction orchestrating fetch → prompt → LLM → parse

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-08-icp-profile-generator-design.md

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should detail exact data fetching (which queries to call for convergence results and identity)
- Define fixture JSON for test cases matching Linear product data
- Implement S001 first or inline the aggregation with TODO markers

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

## Implementation Plan

### Summary

Create `convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.ts` with an LLM-powered ICP profile generator that follows the established lens extractor pattern. The file exports two pure functions (`buildICPPrompt`, `parseICPProfiles`), a system prompt constant (`ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT`), and an `internalAction` that orchestrates fetching product profile data, building a prompt with role and value moment context, calling Claude, and parsing the response into `ICPProfile[]`. Since S001 (aggregateICPInputs) hasn't landed yet, data fetching is inlined with TODO markers for future extraction.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.ts` exports an `internalAction` named `generateICPProfiles` with args `{ productId: v.id("products") }` that returns `{ profiles: ICPProfile[]; execution_time_ms: number }` — verified by TypeScript compilation via `npx tsc --noEmit`
- [ ] AC2: `buildICPPrompt` pure function accepts `(roles: Array<{ name: string; occurrence_count: number; tier_1_count: number; value_moments: Array<{ id: string; name: string; description: string; tier: number }> }>, targetCustomer: string)` and returns a string containing the target customer value and at least one role name and value moment ID — verified by unit test asserting `.toContain()` checks on output
- [ ] AC3: `parseICPProfiles` pure function accepts a JSON string containing 2-3 `ICPProfile` objects and returns `ICPProfile[]` with unique IDs assigned, required fields validated (name, description, value_moment_priorities, activation_triggers, pain_points, success_metrics, confidence) — verified by unit test parsing valid JSON fixtures
- [ ] AC4: `parseICPProfiles` throws an error when given fewer than 2 or more than 3 profiles — verified by unit tests with 1-profile and 4-profile inputs that assert `.toThrow(/2-3/)`
- [ ] AC5: `parseICPProfiles` throws an error when two profiles share identical `value_moment_priorities` moment_id sets — verified by unit test with duplicate priority lists that asserts `.toThrow(/distinct/i)`
- [ ] AC6: `ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT` constant contains instructions for "2-3" personas, role-specific naming guidance, and distinctness requirements — verified by unit test with `.toContain()` assertions
- [ ] AC7: `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.test.ts` exits with code 0 and all tests pass

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Create output types file (dependency: M004-E001-S001)**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts`
   - Define `ValueMomentPriority` interface: `{ moment_id: string; priority: 1 | 2 | 3; relevance_reason: string }`
   - Define `ICPProfile` interface: `{ id: string; name: string; description: string; value_moment_priorities: ValueMomentPriority[]; activation_triggers: string[]; pain_points: string[]; success_metrics: string[]; confidence: number; sources: Array<{ url: string; excerpt: string }> }`
   - Note: Only define the ICP-related types needed for this task. Other output types (ActivationMap, MeasurementSpec) will be added by their respective stories. Import `ValueMomentTier` from `../convergence/types` if needed.

2. **Define ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT constant**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.ts`
   - Module-level `export const ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT` string instructing Claude to:
     - Generate exactly 2-3 distinct ICP profiles as a JSON array
     - Use specific, role-based persona names (e.g., "Engineering Team Lead" not "Power User")
     - Include examples of good vs bad persona names
     - Ensure each persona has different `value_moment_priorities` (not identical moment_id sets)
     - Require all fields: name, description, value_moment_priorities[], activation_triggers[], pain_points[], success_metrics[], confidence (0-1)
     - Return ONLY valid JSON, no commentary

3. **Implement buildICPPrompt pure function**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.ts`
   - Signature: `export function buildICPPrompt(roles: RoleInput[], targetCustomer: string): string`
   - Where `RoleInput` is a local type: `{ name: string; occurrence_count: number; tier_1_count: number; value_moments: Array<{ id: string; name: string; description: string; tier: number }> }`
   - Build user prompt containing:
     - Target customer context line (e.g., "Target customer: B2B SaaS product teams")
     - Role summary section listing each role with occurrence_count and tier_1_count
     - Value moments section grouped by role, each with id, name, tier, and description
   - Return the assembled prompt string

4. **Implement parseICPProfiles pure function**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.ts`
   - Signature: `export function parseICPProfiles(responseText: string): ICPProfile[]`
   - Import `extractJson` from `../lenses/shared`
   - Logic:
     1. Extract JSON via `extractJson(responseText)`
     2. Validate is array: throw if not `Array.isArray`
     3. Validate count: throw if length < 2 or > 3 with message containing "2-3"
     4. Map each item, validating required fields:
        - `name` (string, non-empty)
        - `description` (string, non-empty)
        - `value_moment_priorities` (array, non-empty, each with moment_id + priority + relevance_reason)
        - `activation_triggers` (array)
        - `pain_points` (array)
        - `success_metrics` (array)
        - `confidence` (number, clamp to 0-1 range)
     5. Assign unique ID via `crypto.randomUUID()` to each profile
     6. Validate distinctness: collect moment_id sets per profile, compare sorted joined strings — throw if any two are identical (message containing "distinct")
     7. Return `ICPProfile[]`

5. **Implement generateICPProfiles internalAction**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.ts`
   - Import `internalAction` from `../../_generated/server`, `internal` from `../../_generated/api`, `v` from `convex/values`, `callClaude` from `../lenses/shared`
   - Define `generateICPProfiles` as `internalAction` with args `{ productId: v.id("products") }`
   - Handler logic:
     1. Record `startTime = Date.now()`
     2. Fetch product profile: `await ctx.runQuery(internal.productProfiles.getInternal, { productId: args.productId })`
     3. If no profile, throw `"No product profile found"`
     4. Extract convergence value moments: `(profile.convergence as ConvergenceResult | undefined)?.value_moments ?? []`
     5. Extract target customer: `(profile.identity as { targetCustomer?: string } | undefined)?.targetCustomer ?? ""`
     6. // TODO: Replace inline aggregation with aggregateICPInputs when S001 (basesignal-uri) lands
     7. Build inline role aggregation: group value moments by role (fan-out), compute occurrence_count and tier_1_count per role
     8. Build prompt: `buildICPPrompt(roles, targetCustomer)`
     9. Call Claude: `callClaude({ system: ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user: prompt, temperature: 0.3 })`
     10. Parse response: `parseICPProfiles(responseText)`
     11. Return `{ profiles, execution_time_ms: Date.now() - startTime }`

6. **Write unit tests for ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.test.ts`
   - `describe("ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT")` block:
     - Contains "2-3" for persona count guidance
     - Contains "value_moment_priorities" for required field
     - Contains role-specific naming guidance (e.g., "role" or persona naming examples)
     - Contains "distinct" for distinctness requirement

7. **Write unit tests for buildICPPrompt**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.test.ts`
   - Create `makeRoleInput` helper returning a valid role input object
   - `describe("buildICPPrompt")` block:
     - Includes target customer when non-empty
     - Includes role names from input
     - Includes value moment IDs and names
     - Includes tier information for each moment
     - Handles empty roles array gracefully

8. **Write unit tests for parseICPProfiles**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.test.ts`
   - Create `makeValidProfile` helper returning a valid ICP profile JSON object (without id — parser assigns it)
   - Create `makeValidResponse` helper wrapping profiles in JSON string
   - `describe("parseICPProfiles")` block:
     - **Happy paths:**
       - Parses valid 2-profile response, returns array of length 2
       - Parses valid 3-profile response, returns array of length 3
       - Assigns unique IDs to each profile (truthy, distinct)
       - Handles code-fenced JSON (```json ... ```)
       - Clamps confidence to 0-1 range (1.5 → 1.0, -0.5 → 0)
     - **Count validation:**
       - Throws on 1-profile response with message matching /2-3/
       - Throws on 4-profile response with message matching /2-3/
       - Throws on 0-profile (empty array) response
     - **Field validation:**
       - Throws on missing/empty name
       - Throws on missing/empty description
       - Throws on missing value_moment_priorities
       - Throws on empty value_moment_priorities array
       - Throws on non-array response (object instead of array)
     - **Distinctness validation:**
       - Throws when two profiles have identical moment_id sets (same IDs, same order)
       - Throws when two profiles have identical moment_id sets (same IDs, different order)
       - Passes when profiles share some but not all moment_ids

### Test Plan

- [ ] **AC1**: TypeScript compilation check — `npx tsc --noEmit` passes without errors; `generateICPProfiles` is accessible as `internal.analysis.outputs.generateICPProfiles`
- [ ] **AC2**: Unit test `buildICPPrompt` — given roles with names "PM" and "Engineer" and targetCustomer "B2B SaaS", output `.toContain("B2B SaaS")`, `.toContain("PM")`, `.toContain("Engineer")`, and `.toContain(momentId)`
- [ ] **AC3**: Unit test `parseICPProfiles` — given valid 2-profile JSON, returns array of length 2, each with all required fields populated and unique `id` values assigned
- [ ] **AC4**: Unit test `parseICPProfiles` — given 1-profile JSON, throws error matching `/2-3/`; given 4-profile JSON, throws error matching `/2-3/`
- [ ] **AC5**: Unit test `parseICPProfiles` — given 2 profiles with identical `value_moment_priorities[].moment_id` sets, throws error matching `/distinct/i`
- [ ] **AC6**: Unit test `ICP_SYSTEM_PROMPT` — string contains "2-3", "value_moment_priorities", role naming guidance, and "distinct"
- [ ] **AC7**: Full test suite — `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.test.ts` exits with code 0

---
*Plan created via /plan-issue*

title: M010-E002-S004: Tests for lifecycle states generator
description:
## M010-E002-S004: Tests for Lifecycle States Generator

### Acceptance Criteria
- Test: buildLifecycleStatesPrompt includes product name and business model
- Test: buildLifecycleStatesPrompt includes value moment names
- Test: buildLifecycleStatesPrompt includes activation level names
- Test: parseLifecycleStatesResponse parses valid 7-state JSON
- Test: parseLifecycleStatesResponse throws on invalid JSON
- Test: generateLifecycleStates with mock LLM returns valid LifecycleStatesResult
- Test: generated result has 7+ states matching expected lifecycle states
- Test: generated result has transitions between states

### Proposed Solution
Create `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/__tests__/outputs/lifecycle-states.test.ts` with:
- Inline fixtures: sampleIdentity (B2B SaaS "ProjectBoard"), sampleActivationLevels (2 levels), sampleValueMoments (1 moment), sampleActivationMap (2 stages), VALID_LIFECYCLE_RESPONSE (7-state JSON)
- 3 describe blocks, 5 tests covering 6+ ACs (some combined)
- Inline mock LLM: `{ complete: async () => JSON.stringify(VALID_LIFECYCLE_RESPONSE) } as LlmProvider`

Key decisions:
- File in __tests__/outputs/ (not __tests__/) — matches codebase convention
- Prompt builder uses individual params; generator uses wrapper object (S003 destructures internally)
- Self-contained fixtures, no shared mock-llm deps
- Combined generator assertions into single test (one mock LLM call = one concept)

### Design Doc
docs/plans/2026-02-22-lifecycle-states-generator-tests-design.md
