title: Implement candidate validation pass
description:
## Context

**Mission M003:** 7-Lens Value Discovery Validation
**Epic M003-E002:** Validation & Convergence - Clean and cluster lens output

**Story M003-E002-S002:** Catch common failure modes in lens candidates.

**Dependencies:** Requires S001 (convergence schema) to exist.

## Specification

A validation function reviews lens candidates and catches common failure modes:
feature-as-value, vague candidates, duplicates within lens, role mismatches,
unsupported claims, and hallucinated features. Candidates are marked valid,
rewritten, or removed.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] validateCandidates function accepts LensResult[] and returns ValidatedCandidate[]
2. [unit] Candidates starting with 'Use the...' or 'Click the...' are flagged as feature-as-value
3. [unit] Candidates with vague terms ('better visibility', 'improved efficiency') without specifics are flagged
4. [unit] Duplicate candidates within same lens (similarity >0.85) are merged
5. [unit] Candidates referencing features not in knowledge graph are flagged
6. [integration] Validation reduces total candidate count by 20-40% through removal and merging
7. [integration] Rewritten candidates have outcome-focused descriptions
8. [integration] Each flagged candidate includes validation_issue explaining why

## Guidance

Create convex/analysis/convergence/validateCandidates.ts as an internalAction.

Validation checks: feature-as-value, too vague, duplicates, role mismatch,
unsupported claims, hallucinated features.

Use Claude Haiku for validation prompts (faster, cheaper).

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/lenses/types.ts
- convex/productProfiles.ts

---
*Product Story: M003-E002-S002 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Hallucinated features should be soft-flagged with rewrite, not removed — knowledge graph may be incomplete
- When candidate references unknown feature, rewrite to outcome language rather than feature language
- Flag patterns in metadata for future crawler improvement

### Technical Perspective
- Deterministic-first approach: pure functions for testability (5 of 8 ACs are unit tests)
- Two categories: deterministic checks (regex, similarity, set membership) then LLM for judgment
- Architecture mirrors the cognitive split, easy to evolve thresholds

### Simplification Review
- Collapsed 3-phase pipeline into: single deterministic pass + one LLM call
- Removed intermediate CheckResult type — populate ValidatedCandidate directly
- Embedded prompt building/parsing in orchestrator (not separate exports)
- Removed artificial LLM batch size cap
- LensCandidate/LensResult types belong to upstream story S001

### Final Design
Deterministic checks (isFeatureAsValue, isVagueCandidate, findWithinLensDuplicates, hasUnverifiedFeatureRef) as exported pure functions for unit testing. Single Claude Haiku call for flagged candidates to confirm/override and rewrite to outcome language. One internalAction orchestrator.

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should detail: exact regex patterns, vague phrase list, knowledge graph extraction logic, Haiku prompt template, response parsing, test fixtures

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-07-candidate-validation-design.md

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

---

## Implementation Plan

### Summary

Implement a candidate validation pass that catches common quality issues in 7-lens pipeline output before semantic clustering. Four exported pure functions handle deterministic checks (feature-as-value regex, vague phrase matching, within-lens TF-IDF duplicate detection, unverified feature reference checking), and a single Claude Haiku call rewrites flagged candidates to outcome-focused language. The result is a `ValidatedCandidate[]` that feeds into the existing `clusterCandidates` action.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `convex/analysis/convergence/validateCandidates.ts` exports a `validateCandidates` internalAction that accepts `{ lensResults: v.any(), productId: v.id("products") }` and returns `ValidatedCandidate[]` (from `convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts`)
- [ ] AC2: `isFeatureAsValue("Use the dashboard", "...")` returns a non-null string; `isFeatureAsValue("Gain visibility into...", "...")` returns `null`; patterns include `/^use the\b/i`, `/^click the\b/i`, `/^open the\b/i`, `/^navigate to\b/i`, `/^select the\b/i`, `/^enable\b/i`, `/^toggle\b/i`, `/^turn on\b/i`, `/^activate the\b/i`, `/^go to\b/i`
- [ ] AC3: `isVagueCandidate("Provides better visibility into team performance")` returns non-null; `isVagueCandidate("Reduces deployment time from 45 minutes to 3 minutes by automating CI/CD")` returns `null`; vague phrases: "better visibility", "improved efficiency", "enhanced experience", "streamlined workflow", "greater insights", "increased productivity", "better outcomes", "improved performance", "enhanced capabilities", "seamless integration", "intuitive interface", "powerful features", "actionable insights", "data-driven decisions"
- [ ] AC4: `findWithinLensDuplicates([{id:"a", name:"Track team tasks", description:"..."}, {id:"b", name:"Track team task items", description:"..."}], 0.85)` returns an array with one entry `{keep: "a", remove: "b", similarity: >=0.85}`; candidates from different lenses are never compared (grouping by lens happens in the orchestrator)
- [ ] AC5: `hasUnverifiedFeatureRef("Uses the Gantt chart to visualize timelines", new Set(["dashboard", "kanban board"]))` returns non-null (Gantt chart not in known features); `hasUnverifiedFeatureRef("Uses the dashboard to track progress", new Set(["dashboard"]))` returns `null`
- [ ] AC6: Given a fixture of ~10 candidates including 2 feature-as-value, 2 vague, 1 duplicate pair, and 1 unverified feature ref, the output has fewer candidates than input with reduction in range 20-40% (removal of duplicates + any LLM-confirmed removals)
- [ ] AC7: Candidates with `validation_status: "rewritten"` have `rewritten_from` populated with original `{name, description}` and the new `name`/`description` fields contain outcome-focused language (verified by absence of feature-as-value patterns in rewritten text)
- [ ] AC8: Every candidate with `validation_status` of `"rewritten"` or `"removed"` has a non-empty `validation_issue` string

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Create validateCandidates.ts with deterministic check functions**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/validateCandidates.ts`
   - Export `isFeatureAsValue(name: string, description: string): string | null` — test name AND description against `FEATURE_AS_VALUE_PATTERNS` regex array. Return explanation string if matched, null otherwise.
   - Export `isVagueCandidate(description: string): string | null` — case-insensitive substring match against `VAGUE_PHRASES` array. Return explanation if matched, null otherwise.
   - Export `findWithinLensDuplicates(candidates: Array<{id: string; name: string; description: string}>, threshold?: number): Array<{keep: string; remove: string; similarity: number}>` — use `computeTfIdfVectors` and `cosineSimilarity` from `convex/lib/similarity.ts`. For each pair above threshold, keep the first (by array order) and mark the second for removal.
   - Export `hasUnverifiedFeatureRef(description: string, knownFeatures: Set<string>): string | null` — extract capitalized multi-word noun phrases from description using regex `/[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*/g`, check each against `knownFeatures` (case-insensitive). Return explanation for first unmatched phrase, null if all match.
   - Export `buildKnownFeaturesSet(profile: any): Set<string>` — extract feature names from `profile.entities?.items?.map(e => e.name)` and `profile.outcomes?.items?.flatMap(o => o.linkedFeatures)`. Lowercase all entries.
   - Define `FEATURE_AS_VALUE_PATTERNS` and `VAGUE_PHRASES` constants as specified in the design doc.

2. **Add the orchestrator internalAction**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/validateCandidates.ts`
   - Define a local `LensInput` interface: `{ lens: string; candidates: Array<{ id: string; name: string; description: string; role?: string; confidence?: number; [key: string]: unknown }> }` — this decouples from upstream `LensResult` type that may not exist yet.
   - Export `validateCandidates` internalAction with args `{ lensResults: v.any(), productId: v.id("products") }`.
   - Handler flow:
     a. Load product profile via `ctx.runQuery(internal.productProfiles.getInternal, { productId })`
     b. Build `knownFeatures` set via `buildKnownFeaturesSet(profile)`
     c. Flatten all candidates from `lensResults` into a working array, assigning each a `ValidatedCandidate` shape with initial `validation_status: "valid"`
     d. Run deterministic checks per candidate: `isFeatureAsValue`, `isVagueCandidate`, `hasUnverifiedFeatureRef` — collect flagged candidates
     e. Run `findWithinLensDuplicates` per lens group — mark duplicates as `validation_status: "removed"` with `validation_issue`
     f. Send all flagged (non-removed) candidates to Claude Haiku in a single call for confirmation/override and rewriting
     g. Parse LLM response (JSON array), merge results back: update `validation_status` to `"rewritten"`, populate `rewritten_from`, update `name`/`description` with rewritten versions, set `validation_issue`
     h. Return full `ValidatedCandidate[]`

3. **Implement the Claude Haiku LLM call**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/validateCandidates.ts`
   - Instantiate `new Anthropic()` (uses `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env var)
   - Model: `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`
   - System prompt: instruct LLM to review each flagged candidate, confirm or override the flag, and rewrite flagged candidates to outcome-focused language ("Users achieve X" not "Use the Y feature"). Return JSON array of `{ id: string, action: "confirm_flag" | "override_valid" | "remove", rewritten_name?: string, rewritten_description?: string, validation_issue: string }`
   - If no candidates are flagged, skip the LLM call entirely
   - If LLM call fails, keep deterministic flags as-is (graceful degradation)
   - Parse response using JSON extraction (handle code fences)

4. **Create unit tests for deterministic check functions**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/validateCandidates.test.ts`
   - Test `isFeatureAsValue`:
     - "Use the dashboard" → non-null
     - "Click the settings icon" → non-null
     - "Gain visibility into team velocity" → null
     - "Navigate to reports" → non-null
     - "Reduce deployment time" → null
   - Test `isVagueCandidate`:
     - "Provides better visibility into team work" → non-null
     - "Delivers improved efficiency for managers" → non-null
     - "Reduces deployment from 45min to 3min via CI/CD automation" → null
     - "Actionable insights for decision makers" → non-null
   - Test `findWithinLensDuplicates`:
     - Two near-identical candidates → returns 1 pair with similarity ≥ 0.85
     - Two completely different candidates → returns empty array
     - Three candidates where A≈B but C is different → returns 1 pair
   - Test `hasUnverifiedFeatureRef`:
     - Description with "Gantt Chart", knownFeatures={"dashboard"} → non-null
     - Description with "Dashboard", knownFeatures={"dashboard"} → null
     - Description with no capitalized noun phrases → null
   - Test `buildKnownFeaturesSet`:
     - Profile with entities and outcomes → returns lowercased set of all feature names

5. **Create integration test for the full validation pass**
   - File: `convex/analysis/convergence/validateCandidates.test.ts` (same file)
   - Use `convex-test` to test the `validateCandidates` internalAction
   - Create a fixture of ~10 candidates across 2 lenses:
     - 2 feature-as-value ("Use the Kanban board...", "Click the timeline view...")
     - 2 vague ("Better visibility into...", "Improved efficiency of...")
     - 1 duplicate pair within same lens (high similarity)
     - 1 unverified feature reference
     - 4 clean candidates
   - Assert: output count < input count (reduction)
   - Assert: flagged candidates have `validation_issue` string
   - Assert: rewritten candidates have `rewritten_from` populated
   - Mock the Anthropic API call to return predictable rewrite responses
   - Note: exact 20-40% reduction depends on LLM mock behavior, so test the deterministic checks precisely and the integration flow structurally

### Test Plan

- [ ] AC1: Run `npx vitest run convex/analysis/convergence/validateCandidates.test.ts` — integration test creates `validateCandidates` internalAction call and verifies it returns `ValidatedCandidate[]` with correct shape
- [ ] AC2: Unit test `isFeatureAsValue` with "Use the..." and "Click the..." inputs returns non-null; clean inputs return null
- [ ] AC3: Unit test `isVagueCandidate` with "better visibility" and "improved efficiency" inputs returns non-null; specific descriptions return null
- [ ] AC4: Unit test `findWithinLensDuplicates` with near-identical candidates returns pair with similarity ≥ 0.85
- [ ] AC5: Unit test `hasUnverifiedFeatureRef` with unknown feature returns non-null; known feature returns null
- [ ] AC6: Integration test with 10-candidate fixture asserts output.length < input.length
- [ ] AC7: Integration test asserts candidates with `validation_status: "rewritten"` have `rewritten_from` populated and new description doesn't match feature-as-value patterns
- [ ] AC8: Integration test asserts every non-"valid" candidate has non-empty `validation_issue`

title: M012-E004-S003: MCP Tools and Final Validation
description:
Mission: M012 - Double Three-Layer Measurement Spec
Epic: E004 - Pipeline, CLI, and Storage Integration
Story: S003 - MCP Tools and Final Validation

## What
Update updateDefinition MCP tool, run full test suite, validate with real scan.

## Acceptance Criteria
- updateDefinition.ts recalculateCompleteness works with new measurement_spec shape
- storage/src/types.ts measurement_spec field still works (Record<string, unknown>)
- npm run build succeeds across all packages
- npm run test:run passes across all packages
- No TypeScript errors in any package
- A scan output produces perspectives with product, customer, interaction keys
- Product perspective has 3-7 entities with past-tense activities
- Customer perspective has derived activities with derivation_rule
- Interaction perspective has generic activities
- jsonSchemas array contains one schema per entity

## Files to Modify
- packages/mcp-server/src/tools/updateDefinition.ts
- packages/storage/src/types.ts

## Context
updateDefinition.ts just checks measurement_spec presence (truthy) — minimal change. storage/src/types.ts uses Record<string, unknown> — no change needed. Real scan validation is the final integration test for the entire M012 mission.

## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Verify that the existing `updateDefinition` MCP tool and storage layer work correctly with the new perspective-grouped MeasurementSpec shape introduced by E001-E003. The core completeness check (`!!input.measurement_spec`) and storage type (`Record<string, unknown>`) are both shape-agnostic, so **no logic changes are needed** — this story is about adding regression tests with new-shape fixtures and performing final integration validation via a real scan.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `recalculateCompleteness` in `packages/mcp-server/src/tools/updateDefinition.ts` returns a valid `{ completeness, overallConfidence }` when `profile.measurement_spec` or `profile.outputs.measurement_spec` contains the new perspective-grouped shape (object with `perspectives.product`, `perspectives.customer`, `perspectives.interaction` keys)
- [ ] `computeCompleteness` in `packages/core/src/completeness.ts` marks `measurement_spec` as present when passed a new-shape object (truthy check `!!input.measurement_spec` works for `{ perspectives: {...}, jsonSchemas: [...] }`)
- [ ] `packages/storage/src/types.ts` `measurement_spec?: Record<string, unknown>` accepts the new shape without TypeScript errors (no code change needed — verify only)
- [ ] Test in `packages/core/src/completeness.test.ts`: new test case passes a perspectives-shaped measurement_spec and asserts `measurement_spec` section is `present: true`
- [ ] Test in `packages/mcp-server/src/tools/updateDefinition.test.ts`: new test case creates a profile with `measurement_spec` containing new perspective-grouped shape, calls `handleUpdateDefinition`, and verifies completeness is recalculated without errors
- [ ] Test in `packages/mcp-server/src/tools/updateDefinition.test.ts`: new test case creates a profile with `outputs.measurement_spec` containing new shape, calls `handleUpdateDefinition`, and verifies completeness includes measurement_spec as present
- [ ] `npm run build` exits with code 0 across all packages
- [ ] `npm run test:run` exits with code 0 across all packages
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` reports no TypeScript errors in any package
- [ ] A real scan (`npx basesignal scan <url>`) produces output JSON with `perspectives` containing `product`, `customer`, `interaction` keys
- [ ] Product perspective in scan output has 3-7 entities with past-tense activity names
- [ ] Customer perspective in scan output has activities with `derivation_rule` field
- [ ] Interaction perspective in scan output has generic activities
- [ ] Scan output contains `jsonSchemas` array with one schema per entity

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Add new-shape completeness regression test**
   - File: `packages/core/src/completeness.test.ts`
   - Add test: `it("marks measurement_spec present with perspective-grouped shape")` that passes `{ perspectives: { product: { entities: [] }, customer: { entities: [] }, interaction: { entities: [] } }, jsonSchemas: [], confidence: 0.8, sources: [] }` and asserts `measurement_spec` section is `present: true` and completeness includes it

2. **Add new-shape updateDefinition regression test (top-level measurement_spec)**
   - File: `packages/mcp-server/src/tools/updateDefinition.test.ts`
   - Add test: `it("recalculates completeness with perspective-grouped measurement_spec")` that:
     - Creates a profile via `makeTestProfile({ id: "prod-ms", measurement_spec: { perspectives: { product: { entities: [{ id: "board", name: "Board", ... }] }, customer: { entities: [] }, interaction: { entities: [] } }, jsonSchemas: [], confidence: 0.8, sources: [] } })`
     - Saves it via MockStorage
     - Calls `handleUpdateDefinition` to update identity (triggers recalculateCompleteness)
     - Asserts result is not an error
     - Loads saved profile and verifies `completeness` is > 0 and `measurement_spec` data persists

3. **Add new-shape updateDefinition regression test (outputs.measurement_spec)**
   - File: `packages/mcp-server/src/tools/updateDefinition.test.ts`
   - Add test: `it("recalculates completeness with measurement_spec in outputs")` that:
     - Creates a profile with `outputs: { measurement_spec: { perspectives: {...}, jsonSchemas: [], confidence: 0.8, sources: [] } }`
     - Calls `handleUpdateDefinition` to update identity
     - Asserts completeness recalculation succeeds without errors

4. **Verify storage type compatibility (no code change)**
   - File: `packages/storage/src/types.ts`
   - Confirm `measurement_spec?: Record<string, unknown>` — no changes needed
   - This is verified implicitly by step 2 (MockStorage uses the same type)

5. **Run full build**
   - Command: `npm run build`
   - Expected: exits with code 0

6. **Run full test suite**
   - Command: `npm run test:run`
   - Expected: exits with code 0, all tests pass

7. **Run TypeScript check**
   - Command: `npx tsc --noEmit` (or equivalent per-package)
   - Expected: no errors

8. **Run real scan and validate output structure**
   - Command: `npx basesignal scan https://amplitude.com` (or similar public product)
   - Validate output file (`~/.basesignal/products/<slug>/measurement-spec.json`):
     - Has `perspectives` key with `product`, `customer`, `interaction` sub-keys
     - Product has 3-7 entities with past-tense activity names (e.g., "board_created")
     - Customer has activities with `derivation_rule` (e.g., "Board shared (first time) OR ...")
     - Interaction has generic activities (e.g., "element_clicked", "element_submitted")
     - `jsonSchemas` array contains one JSON Schema per entity across all perspectives

### Test Plan
- [ ] `npm test -- --run` in `packages/core` — completeness test with new shape passes, `measurement_spec` section marked `present: true`
- [ ] `npm test -- --run` in `packages/mcp-server` — updateDefinition test with top-level `measurement_spec` (new shape) succeeds, completeness > 0
- [ ] `npm test -- --run` in `packages/mcp-server` — updateDefinition test with `outputs.measurement_spec` (new shape) succeeds without errors
- [ ] `npm run build` — exits with code 0
- [ ] `npm run test:run` — all packages pass
- [ ] `npx basesignal scan <url>` — output JSON has `perspectives.product.entities` (3-7), `perspectives.customer.entities[0].activities[*].derivation_rule`, `perspectives.interaction.entities[0].activities`, `jsonSchemas` (length = total entity count)

title: M010-E001-S002: Create Zod validation schemas for lifecycle types
description:
## M010-E001-S002: Create Zod Validation Schemas for Lifecycle Types

### Acceptance Criteria
- StateCriterionSchema validates: event_name (string min 1), condition (string min 1), threshold (optional number)
- LifecycleStateSchema validates: name, definition, entry_criteria (array of StateCriterion), exit_triggers (array of string), time_window (optional string)
- StateTransitionSchema validates: from_state (min 1), to_state (min 1), trigger_conditions (array of string), typical_timeframe (optional string)
- LifecycleStatesResultSchema validates: states[], transitions[], confidence (number), sources (string[])
- Schema rejects invalid input (missing required fields, wrong types)
- All four schemas exported from schema/outputs.ts

### Proposed Solution
Add `// --- Lifecycle States ---` section to `packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts` after User State Model section (after line ~108), before Tracking Event section. Follow existing file conventions: z.object(), .min(1), .optional(), z.infer type exports after each schema.

Key decisions:
- StateCriterionSchema is standalone — NOT extending UserStateCriterionSchema
- exit_triggers uses z.array(z.string()), NOT z.array(StateCriterionSchema) — narrative vs structured
- Include z.infer type exports to match file convention
- Story TOML says exit_triggers is "array of StateCriterion" but design doc corrects to "array of string"

### Files to Modify
- `packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts` — add 4 schemas + 4 inferred types

### Design Doc
docs/plans/2026-02-22-lifecycle-zod-schemas-design.md

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:
## Auto-Brainstorming Complete (re-run)

### Product Perspective
- Lifecycle states schemas are purely structural — mirror the TypeScript types for runtime validation
- Downstream consumers (response parser, pipeline) depend on these for input validation

### Technical Perspective
- StateCriterionSchema is standalone (not extending UserStateCriterionSchema) — they are parallel domain concepts that coincidentally share event_name + condition fields
- Coupling them would create a false subtype relationship and risk future breakage
- `exit_triggers` uses `z.array(z.string())`, NOT `z.array(StateCriterionSchema)` — exit triggers are narrative descriptions (human-readable), entry criteria are structured (machine-checkable), matching the TypeScript types design and existing `ActivationStage.trigger_events` pattern
- `trigger_conditions` in StateTransitionSchema also uses `z.array(z.string())` for the same reason
- Follow existing file conventions exactly: z.object(), .min(1), .optional(), z.array(), z.infer
- Scope boundary: this task defines schemas in outputs.ts only; barrel re-exports through schema/index.ts belong to basesignal-34m

### Simplification Review
- Verdict: APPROVED — design is minimal, nothing to remove or simplify
- Entry/exit asymmetry (structured vs narrative) is deliberate and well-justified
- No false type hierarchies or unnecessary coupling
- Each schema does exactly one thing; no simpler representation would work

### Final Design
4 Zod schemas added after User State Model section in outputs.ts. Standalone definitions following established patterns. No inheritance, no abstractions, no new concepts — pure mechanical translation of TypeScript interfaces into Zod validation. No barrel re-export changes (basesignal-34m's scope).

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should specify exact insertion point (after line 108), verify no naming conflicts with existing StageTransitionSchema, and confirm npm run build passes.

**Design doc:** docs/plans/2026-02-22-lifecycle-zod-schemas-design.md

---

title: Suggest metrics catalog based on business model
description:
## Context

**Mission M001:** Validate MCP-first product profile generation from website crawling
**Epic M001-E001:** Core Analysis Engine - LLM extraction into product profile

**Story M001-E001-S006:** Suggest relevant metrics categorized by reach/engagement/retention/revenue/value.

**Dependencies:** Requires S001 (identity) and S002 (revenue) to be complete — uses businessModel and revenue.model to select appropriate metric templates.

## Specification

A Convex action takes the already-extracted identity and revenue data and suggests relevant metrics categorized by reach/engagement/retention/revenue/value — with formulas where standard and links to relevant definitions and journey stages.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] Metrics suggested as array of { name, category, formula?, linkedTo[] }
2. [unit] Categories include reach, engagement, retention, revenue, and value
3. [unit] Standard metric formulas included (e.g., 'Activation Rate = activated users / signups')
4. [unit] Metrics are linked to relevant definitions or journey stages via linkedTo[]
5. [unit] Business model type influences which metrics are suggested (PLG vs sales-led vs marketplace)
6. [integration] Result stored via updateSection(productId, 'metrics', data) matching schema

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** `docs/plans/2026-02-04-suggest-metrics-catalog-impl.md`

### Overview
Pure-code internalAction with two extracted pure functions (classifyArchetype, selectMetrics) testable without Convex runtime. Reads profile via getInternal, classifies archetype, filters hardcoded METRIC_CATALOG (~16 metrics), stores via updateSectionInternal.

### Tasks
6 TDD tasks covering:
- Add getInternal query to productProfiles
- Add updateSectionInternal mutation to productProfiles
- Create classifyArchetype pure function + types (10 unit tests)
- Create METRIC_CATALOG + selectMetrics function (10 unit tests)
- Create suggestMetrics internalAction (6 integration tests)
- Full test suite verification

### Testing
- [ ] Run project test suite to verify changes

title: Run comparison and validate H6 hypothesis
description:
## Context

**Mission M003:** 7-Lens Value Discovery Validation
**Epic M003-E003:** Reference Comparison - Validate against known product analysis

**Story M003-E003-S002:** Compare generated moments against reference and validate H6.

**Dependencies:** Requires S001 (Linear reference) to exist. Also requires E001 and E002 complete.

## Specification

Generated value moments for Linear are compared against the reference analysis.
Each Tier 1 moment is rated for accuracy. If 70%+ are accurate or mostly accurate,
H6 is validated and HYPOTHESES.md is updated.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [manual] runAllLenses executed on Linear product with results captured
2. [manual] runConvergencePipeline executed with lens results
3. [manual] Each Tier 1 moment rated: accurate / mostly accurate / inaccurate
4. [manual] Accuracy calculation: (accurate + mostly_accurate) / total >= 70%
5. [manual] Tier 1 moments are recognizable as 'obviously important'
6. [manual] docs/plans/M003-validation-results.md documents comparison findings
7. [manual] HYPOTHESES.md H6 status updated based on validation result
8. [manual] If invalidated: learnings documented with lens-specific refinement opportunities

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Validate hypothesis cheaply before investing in Convex infrastructure (E001/E002)
- Building the full pipeline first is solving the wrong problem -- validate H6 with a lightweight script
- If H6 fails, we save enormous infrastructure investment; if it succeeds, we build with conviction

### Technical Perspective
- Single monolithic script (scripts/validate-h6.mjs) + separate reference doc
- Simple Jaccard word similarity for convergence, not LLM clustering -- transparent and debuggable
- No new Convex infrastructure needed beyond a test query for crawled pages
- Run lenses in parallel (Promise.allSettled) for speed

### Simplification Review
- No modular script directory -- one file is enough for throwaway validation
- No embedding-based similarity -- Jaccard is sufficient
- Script provides first-pass ratings; human makes final judgment
- Does NOT auto-update HYPOTHESES.md -- manual human decision

### Final Design
Single monolithic script that: loads Linear data from Convex, runs 7 lens prompts via Claude API in parallel, validates/filters candidates, clusters by Jaccard similarity with same-lens constraint, tiers by convergence count, compares Tier 1 moments against reference doc, outputs validation report to docs/plans/M003-validation-results.md.

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover:
1. Create docs/reference/linear-value-moments.md (or confirm S001 task does this)
2. Add listByProductForTest query to convex/crawledPages.ts
3. Write scripts/validate-h6.mjs with inline lens prompts
4. Test run against Linear and review output

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-07-validate-h6-comparison-design.md

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

## Implementation Plan

### Summary

Create a throwaway validation script (`scripts/validate-h6.mjs`) that loads Linear crawled pages from Convex, runs 7 analytical lens prompts via Claude API in parallel, clusters candidates by Jaccard word similarity, tiers by convergence count, compares Tier 1 moments against a human-authored reference document, and outputs a validation report. This validates H6 (multi-lens analysis produces accurate value discovery) before investing in full Convex pipeline infrastructure.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `convex/crawledPages.ts` exports a `listByProductForTest` query that returns all crawled pages for a given productId without auth checks, following the same pattern as `productProfiles.getForTest` (line 201 of `convex/productProfiles.ts`)
- [ ] AC2: `docs/reference/linear-value-moments.md` exists and contains 8-15 human-authored value moments for Linear, each with name and description, covering the major value areas (project management, issue tracking, cycles/sprints, triage, integrations, etc.)
- [ ] AC3: `scripts/validate-h6.mjs` exists and is executable via `node scripts/validate-h6.mjs`. It loads Linear data from Convex using ConvexHttpClient + `api.crawledPages.listByProductForTest`, runs 7 lens prompts via `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, and outputs to stdout and `docs/plans/M003-validation-results.md`
- [ ] AC4: The script runs all 7 lenses (Capability Mapping, Effort Elimination, Information Asymmetry, Decision Enablement, State Transitions, Time Compression, Artifact Creation) in parallel using `Promise.allSettled`, each returning a JSON array of value moment candidates with `{name, description, roles[], product_surfaces[]}`
- [ ] AC5: The script clusters candidates using Jaccard word similarity (threshold ~0.35) with same-lens constraint (candidates from same lens never merge), then tiers: 5+ lenses = Tier 1, 3-4 = Tier 2, 1-2 = Tier 3
- [ ] AC6: The script compares each Tier 1 moment against the reference doc by Jaccard similarity, prints the best-match score, and suggests a first-pass rating (accurate / mostly accurate / inaccurate) based on similarity thresholds
- [ ] AC7: `docs/plans/M003-validation-results.md` is generated with sections: per-lens candidate counts, clustering stats, tier distribution, Tier 1 moment detail table (name, best reference match, similarity score, suggested rating), accuracy calculation, and H6 verdict section (instructions for human to update HYPOTHESES.md)
- [ ] AC8: `npm test` exits with code 0 (no regressions from the new Convex query)

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Add listByProductForTest query to Convex**
   - File: `convex/crawledPages.ts`
   - Change: Add a `listByProductForTest` public query (no auth) that queries `crawledPages` by `by_product` index, following the exact pattern of `productProfiles.getForTest` at line 201 of `convex/productProfiles.ts`. Uses `query` (not `internalQuery`) so scripts can call it via ConvexHttpClient.

2. **Create Linear reference value moments document**
   - File: `docs/reference/linear-value-moments.md`
   - Change: Author 8-15 reference value moments for Linear based on public knowledge of the product. Each moment has a name and 1-2 sentence description. Cover: issue tracking workflow, project management, cycles/sprints, triage/inbox, custom views/filtering, integrations (GitHub/Slack), keyboard-driven speed, team workspaces, roadmap planning, and initiative tracking. This serves as the comparison baseline.

3. **Write the monolithic validation script**
   - File: `scripts/validate-h6.mjs`
   - Change: Create a single-file ESM script with these inline sections:
     - **Config**: Convex URL (hardcoded dev URL `https://woozy-kangaroo-701.convex.cloud`), Linear product ID (`nh78n36ve9dt5q0z2pnj9gadnx80mm54`), similarity threshold (0.35), tier thresholds (5, 3)
     - **Data loading**: ConvexHttpClient fetches crawled pages via `api.crawledPages.listByProductForTest` and profile via `api.productProfiles.getForTest`
     - **7 lens prompts**: Inline prompt strings for each lens, each instructing Claude to return a JSON array of `{name, description, roles, product_surfaces}`. Batch 1 (Capability Mapping, Effort Elimination, Time Compression, Artifact Creation) runs independently; Batch 2 (Information Asymmetry, Decision Enablement, State Transitions) receives Batch 1 results as additional context
     - **Claude API calls**: Uses `@anthropic-ai/sdk` with `claude-haiku-4-20250414` model, `Promise.allSettled` for parallel execution within each batch
     - **Validation pass**: Simple heuristic filters — remove candidates with names < 3 words that look like feature names, remove candidates with vague descriptions (< 15 chars), deduplicate by exact name match within same lens
     - **Clustering**: Jaccard word similarity (tokenize on whitespace + lowercase, threshold 0.35), greedy single-linkage with same-lens constraint, using inline tokenize/jaccard functions (not importing from convex/lib/similarity.ts since this is a standalone script)
     - **Tiering**: Count distinct lenses per cluster. 5+ = Tier 1, 3-4 = Tier 2, 1-2 = Tier 3. Name each moment from the candidate with the longest description in the cluster
     - **Reference comparison**: Parse `docs/reference/linear-value-moments.md`, for each Tier 1 moment find the reference moment with highest Jaccard similarity on (name + description), suggest rating based on score (>0.3 = accurate, >0.15 = mostly accurate, else inaccurate)
     - **Report generation**: Write structured markdown to `docs/plans/M003-validation-results.md`

4. **Run the validation and capture results**
   - File: `docs/plans/M003-validation-results.md` (generated)
   - Change: Execute `node scripts/validate-h6.mjs` and verify it produces the report file. Review output for sanity (60-140 total candidates, 4-8 Tier 1 moments, report has all expected sections). This is a manual execution step.

5. **Verify no test regressions**
   - File: (all test files)
   - Change: Run `npm test` to ensure the new `listByProductForTest` query doesn't break existing tests. No new test file needed for the script itself (throwaway validation code), but the Convex query addition must not cause regressions.

### Test Plan

- [ ] TP1 (for AC1): After adding `listByProductForTest`, run `npx convex dev` to verify it syncs without errors. Run `npm test` to verify no regressions.
- [ ] TP2 (for AC2): Manually verify `docs/reference/linear-value-moments.md` contains 8-15 named value moments with descriptions.
- [ ] TP3 (for AC3-AC7): Run `node scripts/validate-h6.mjs` and verify:
  - Script completes without errors
  - Stdout shows per-lens candidate counts (expect 60-140 total)
  - Stdout shows cluster count and tier distribution
  - `docs/plans/M003-validation-results.md` exists and contains all sections: lens stats, clustering stats, tier distribution, Tier 1 detail table, accuracy calculation, H6 verdict instructions
- [ ] TP4 (for AC8): `npm test` exits with code 0

---

title: Aggregate role and value moment data for ICP generation
description:
## Context

**Mission M004:** Actionable Output Generation
**Epic M004-E002:** ICP Profile Generation

**Story M004-E002-S001:** Aggregate role and value moment data for ICP generation

**Dependencies:** Requires M004-E001-S001 (output types)

## Specification

A helper function aggregates all role mentions from value moments, lens candidates, and identity extraction to provide input data for the ICP generator.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] aggregateICPInputs function accepts productId and returns ICPInputData
2. [unit] Collects all unique roles from ValueMoment.roles[]
3. [unit] Includes target_customer from identity extraction
4. [unit] Groups value moments by role affinity
5. [integration] Linear test returns 3+ distinct roles with associated value moments

### Reference Files
- convex/analysis/convergence/types.ts
- convex/analysis/extractIdentity.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Fan-out grouping is essential: value moments appearing under ALL their roles preserves multi-role signals for buying committee dynamics
- Hiding multi-role signals produces weaker personas that miss how buyers make decisions together

### Technical Perspective
- Pure core + thin wrapper pattern matches existing codebase conventions (urlUtils, metricSuggestions)
- Use internalQuery (not action) since the wrapper only reads from DB
- Core function takes (valueMoments, targetCustomer) — explicit inputs, no Convex context needed
- Data sources: only convergence + identity (lens candidates are intermediate, already folded into ValueMoment.roles[])

### Simplification Review
- Reviewer questioned whether aggregation function is needed at all (could inline into consumers)
- Kept because: story ACs require it, enables clean unit testing, fan-out + sort + targetCustomer logic is non-trivial
- Removed: over-defensive phantom role creation for empty targetCustomer
- Simplified: role normalization is just .trim()

### Final Design
Single file `convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.ts`:
- Types: RoleAggregation, ICPInputData
- Pure function: aggregateICPInputsCore(valueMoments, targetCustomer) — fan-out groups, sorts by tier-1 count
- Convex wrapper: aggregateICPInputs internalQuery — reads profile, calls core

Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-08-icp-input-aggregation-design.md

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: file creation, type definitions, core function, wrapper, unit tests, integration test
- Follow pure core + wrapper pattern from existing helpers

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

---

## Implementation Plan

### Summary

Create `convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.ts` with a pure core function that fan-out groups value moments by role and a thin Convex `internalQuery` wrapper that reads from the product profile. This bridges raw convergence data and identity extraction into structured role-grouped input for the downstream ICP profile generator (M004-E002-S002).

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: `convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.ts` exports `aggregateICPInputsCore` which accepts `(valueMoments: ValueMoment[], targetCustomer: string)` and returns an `ICPInputData` object with `roles: RoleAggregation[]`, `target_customer: string`, and `total_value_moments: number`
- [ ] AC2: Given 3 value moments with roles `["PM", "Engineer"]`, `["PM"]`, `["Designer"]`, calling `aggregateICPInputsCore` returns roles array containing entries for "PM" (2 moments), "Engineer" (1 moment), and "Designer" (1 moment) — each role's `value_moments[]` includes all moments that mention that role (fan-out)
- [ ] AC3: When `targetCustomer` is non-empty (e.g., `"B2B SaaS product teams"`) and not already present as a role name, `aggregateICPInputsCore` includes it in the `roles[]` array with `occurrence_count: 0` and empty `value_moments[]`. When `targetCustomer` is empty string, it is NOT added as a phantom role
- [ ] AC4: `roles[]` array is sorted by `tier_1_moments` descending, then `occurrence_count` descending — roles with more tier-1 value moments appear first
- [ ] AC5: `convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.ts` exports an `internalQuery` named `aggregateICPInputs` that accepts `{ productId: Id<"products"> }`, reads the product profile via `by_product` index, extracts `convergence.value_moments` and `identity.targetCustomer`, calls `aggregateICPInputsCore`, and returns the result
- [ ] AC6: `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.test.ts` passes with all tests green, covering: fan-out grouping, role deduplication with `.trim()`, targetCustomer inclusion/exclusion, sorting order, empty inputs edge case, and integration test producing 3+ distinct roles

### Implementation Tasks

1. **Create type definitions**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.ts`
   - Define `RoleAggregation` interface: `{ name: string; occurrence_count: number; value_moments: ValueMoment[]; tier_1_moments: number }`
   - Define `ICPInputData` interface: `{ roles: RoleAggregation[]; target_customer: string; total_value_moments: number }`
   - Import `ValueMoment` from `../../analysis/convergence/types`

2. **Implement pure core function `aggregateICPInputsCore`**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.ts`
   - Signature: `export function aggregateICPInputsCore(valueMoments: ValueMoment[], targetCustomer: string): ICPInputData`
   - Logic:
     1. Create `Map<string, ValueMoment[]>` for role→moments mapping
     2. Iterate each value moment; for each `role` in `vm.roles`, normalize with `.trim()`, skip empty strings, and append the moment to that role's array (fan-out)
     3. If `targetCustomer.trim()` is non-empty and not already a key in the map, add it with empty array
     4. Convert map to `RoleAggregation[]`: for each entry, compute `occurrence_count` (array length) and `tier_1_moments` (count where `vm.tier === 1`)
     5. Sort by `tier_1_moments` desc, then `occurrence_count` desc
     6. Return `{ roles, target_customer: targetCustomer, total_value_moments: valueMoments.length }`

3. **Implement Convex internalQuery wrapper**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.ts`
   - Import `internalQuery` from `../_generated/server`, `v` from `convex/values`, `internal` from `../_generated/api`
   - Define `aggregateICPInputs` as `internalQuery` with args `{ productId: v.id("products") }`
   - Handler logic:
     1. Query profile: `ctx.db.query("productProfiles").withIndex("by_product", q => q.eq("productId", args.productId)).first()`
     2. If no profile, return `{ roles: [], target_customer: "", total_value_moments: 0 }`
     3. Extract value moments: cast `profile.convergence` as `ConvergenceResult`, get `.value_moments` (default `[]`)
     4. Extract target customer: cast `profile.identity` as `{ targetCustomer?: string }`, get `.targetCustomer` (default `""`)
     5. Return `aggregateICPInputsCore(valueMoments, targetCustomer)`

4. **Write unit tests for pure core function**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.test.ts`
   - Create `makeValueMoment` helper function following the pattern in `convergeAndTier.test.ts` — returns a `ValueMoment` with sensible defaults and accepts overrides
   - Test cases:
     - `aggregateICPInputsCore` with multi-role moments verifies fan-out (same moment appears under multiple roles)
     - Collects unique roles via `.trim()` normalization (` PM ` and `PM` merge into one)
     - Empty `targetCustomer` is NOT added as phantom role
     - Non-empty `targetCustomer` not already in roles IS added with `occurrence_count: 0`
     - Non-empty `targetCustomer` already in roles is NOT duplicated
     - Sorting: roles with more tier-1 moments sort first; tiebreaker is occurrence_count
     - Empty value moments array returns `{ roles: [], target_customer, total_value_moments: 0 }`
     - Value moments with empty roles arrays are counted in `total_value_moments` but create no role entries

5. **Write integration test with realistic data**
   - File: `convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.test.ts` (same file, separate `describe` block)
   - Create a "Linear-like" fixture with 5-6 value moments spanning roles: "Product Manager", "Engineering Lead", "Designer", "Team Lead"
   - Verify: result has 4 distinct roles, each with correct moment counts
   - Verify: tier-1 heavy roles appear first in the sorted output
   - This validates AC5 (3+ distinct roles) using the pure function directly (no Convex runtime needed for the core logic)

### Test Plan

- [ ] **AC1**: Unit test creates `aggregateICPInputsCore` with valid inputs → returns object matching `ICPInputData` shape with `roles`, `target_customer`, and `total_value_moments` fields
- [ ] **AC2**: Unit test with 3 moments (roles `["PM","Engineer"]`, `["PM"]`, `["Designer"]`) → "PM" role has 2 moments, "Engineer" has 1, "Designer" has 1; PM's `value_moments` array contains both the first and second moments (fan-out verified)
- [ ] **AC3**: Unit test with `targetCustomer="B2B SaaS teams"` not matching any role → roles includes entry with name "B2B SaaS teams" and `occurrence_count: 0`. Separate test with `targetCustomer=""` → no phantom role added
- [ ] **AC4**: Unit test with varied tier distributions → roles array order matches tier_1_moments desc, then occurrence_count desc
- [ ] **AC5**: Wrapper exports verified by TypeScript compilation (`npx tsc --noEmit` passes without errors on the new file)
- [ ] **AC6**: `npm run test:run -- convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.test.ts` exits with code 0 and all tests pass

---
*Plan created via /plan-issue*

title: Detect and classify help/docs subdomains for crawling
description:
## Context

**Mission M002:** Discover and model multi-level activation from product content
**Epic M002-E002:** Enhanced Data Sources for Activation Discovery

**Story M002-E002-S001:** Detect and classify help/docs subdomains for crawling

**Dependencies:** None (first story in epic)

## Specification

The URL classifier recognizes help center subdomains (help.*, docs.*, support.*)
and classifies them appropriately. The shouldCrawl function is updated to allow
these subdomains to be crawled when explicitly requested for activation analysis.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] classifyPageType returns 'help' for help.example.com URLs
2. [unit] classifyPageType returns 'docs' for docs.example.com URLs
3. [unit] classifyPageType returns 'support' for support.example.com URLs
4. [unit] New function shouldCrawlForActivation returns true for help/docs subdomains
5. [unit] shouldCrawlForActivation filters to getting-started/onboarding paths within docs sites
6. [integration] filterHighValuePages includes help.miro.com in docsUrls when scanning miro.com

## Guidance

Current state: shouldCrawl() explicitly skips help.* and support.* subdomains.

Changes needed:
1. Add new page types to classifyPageType for subdomain detection:
   - help.* → 'help'
   - docs.* → 'docs'
   - support.* → 'support'

2. Create new function shouldCrawlForActivation(url):
   - Returns true for help/docs/support subdomains
   - Filters to high-value paths within those sites:
     - /getting-started, /onboarding, /first-steps
     - /tutorials, /quick-start
     - Avoid deep reference docs initially

3. Update filterHighValuePages to optionally use shouldCrawlForActivation
   when crawling for activation analysis (new parameter or separate function).

Keep shouldCrawl() unchanged - it's for marketing page crawling.
Create parallel path for activation-focused crawling.

### Reference Files
- convex/lib/urlUtils.ts

---
*Product Story: M002-E002-S001 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

---

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** `docs/plans/2026-02-05-help-docs-subdomain-detection-impl.md`

### Overview
Extend URL classification with subdomain-based page types, add shouldCrawlForActivation for activation filtering, and update filterHighValuePages to return docsUrls array.

### Tasks
9 TDD tasks covering:
- classifyPageType subdomain detection (help, docs, support)
- shouldCrawlForActivation function with activation path patterns
- filterHighValuePages docsUrls array return type
- scanning.ts caller update
- Integration test for Miro-style scanning

### Testing
- [ ] Run `npm test -- --run` to verify all changes

---

title: M005-E003-S002: Build ActivationMapSection component
description:
## Story: M005-E003-S002
**Mission:** M005 - Product Profile View
**Epic:** M005-E003 - ICP Profiles & Activation Map

## Outcome
An ActivationMapSection component displays activation stages as horizontal progression cards sorted by level. Each stage shows signal strength, trigger events, value moments unlocked, and drop-off risk. Primary activation level is highlighted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [unit] Stages render as horizontal progression cards sorted by level
- [unit] Each stage shows level + name, signal strength badge (color-coded)
- [unit] Trigger events and value moments unlocked display on each stage
- [unit] Drop-off risk indicator renders as green/amber/red
- [unit] Transition connectors between stages show trigger events and timeframe
- [unit] Primary activation level highlighted with distinct border/background
- [unit] Handles drop_off_risk as both { level, reason } object and string defensively
- [unit] Empty state renders when no activation map exists

## Implementation Hints
Create src/components/product-profile/ActivationMapSection.tsx. Sort stages by level. Flex row with overflow-x-auto. Primary stage: ring-2 ring-indigo-500. Signal strength colors: very_strong=indigo, strong=green, medium=amber, weak=gray. Drop-off risk: normalize defensively for both string and { level, reason } shapes.

## Reference Files
- convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts
- convex/analysis/extractActivationLevels.ts
- src/components/ui/card.tsx
- src/components/ui/badge.tsx

---
*Product Story: M005-E003-S002 · Created via /product-story-handoff*

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:
## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Technical Perspective
- Horizontal layout with flex row + overflow-x-auto matches progression mental model
- Keep normalizeDropOffRisk in-file (defensive for both string and {level, reason} shapes per AC)
- Use existing Card and Badge components, pure presentation with no Convex queries
- Transition lookup via simple .find() — only 3-5 stages, clarity over optimization

### Simplification Review
- Removed extracted sub-components (StageCard, TransitionConnector) — inline everything in the map loop
- Replaced transition lookup Map with .find() for clarity
- Single file target: ~80-100 lines
- Kept normalizeDropOffRisk despite reviewer pushback — acceptance criteria explicitly require defensive handling

### Final Design
Single-file pure presentation component. Horizontal scrolling row of Card components with inline SVG arrow connectors between them. Signal strength and risk badges color-coded. Primary activation level highlighted with ring-2 ring-indigo-500. All helpers kept in-file.

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover: component file structure, test setup with mock ActivationMap data, and integration point with ProductProfilePage tabs.

---
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:
## Implementation Plan

### Prerequisites
- **Dependency:** basesignal-ohe (ProductProfilePage) — but ActivationMapSection is standalone and can be built/tested independently as a pure presentation component.
- Uses existing Card/Badge UI components and ActivationMap types from `convex/analysis/outputs/types.ts`

### Step 1: Ensure types re-export
`src/components/product-profile/types.ts` — add `ActivationMap`, `ActivationStage`, `StageTransition` re-exports. Create file if not exists.

### Step 2: Write tests (TDD)
`src/components/product-profile/ActivationMapSection.test.tsx` — 8 tests mapped 1:1 to acceptance criteria:
1. Stages sorted by level in horizontal layout
2. Level + name and signal strength badge with correct color
3. Trigger events and value moments render on each stage
4. Drop-off risk badge renders green/amber/red
5. Transition connectors show trigger events and timeframe
6. Primary activation level gets ring-indigo-500 highlight
7. Object-shaped drop_off_risk `{ level, reason }` handled defensively
8. Empty state when activationMap is null

Test data fixture has stages deliberately out of order (2, 1, 3) to verify sorting. Setup function: `setup({ activationMap })`. No Convex mocking needed (pure presentation).

### Step 3: Build component
`src/components/product-profile/ActivationMapSection.tsx` (~80-100 lines)

- Props: `{ activationMap: ActivationMap | null | undefined }`
- Empty state guard → "No activation map available"
- Sort stages by level ascending
- Flex row with `overflow-x-auto`, min-w-[240px] shrink-0 cards
- Signal strength badge colors: very_strong=indigo, strong=green, medium=amber, weak=gray
- Drop-off risk badge colors: low=green, medium=amber, high=red
- Primary stage: `ring-2 ring-indigo-500 bg-indigo-50`
- Transition connectors between cards via `.find()` on transitions array
- In-file `normalizeDropOffRisk(risk: unknown)` helper for defensive {level, reason} handling
- No extracted sub-components — everything inlined

### Step 4: Verify
`npm test -- --run ActivationMapSection` — all 8 tests pass
`npm run lint` — no errors

### Files to Create
1. `src/components/product-profile/ActivationMapSection.tsx` (~80-100 lines)
2. `src/components/product-profile/ActivationMapSection.test.tsx` (~100 lines)

### Files to Modify

### Dependencies

- No changes to existing files beyond types.ts

title: M007-E001-S001: Rewrite Batch 1 lens prompts for experiential extraction
description:
## Story: M007-E001-S001

**Mission:** M007 - Insight Quality
**Epic:** M007-E001 - Experiential Lens Extraction

## Outcome
The four Batch 1 lenses (time_compression, effort_elimination, capability_mapping, artifact_creation) produce candidates grounded in what users DO inside the product. Each prompt asks about specific user actions, screens, and visible results — not abstract business outcomes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- TIME_COMPRESSION_SYSTEM_PROMPT core question reframed to ask about specific user actions that became instant/near-instant
- EFFORT_ELIMINATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT reframed to ask about specific steps users SKIP
- CAPABILITY_MAPPING_SYSTEM_PROMPT reframed to ask about specific actions a user takes
- ARTIFACT_CREATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT reframed to ask about specific things users BUILD/EXPORT
- Each prompt includes anti-patterns for marketing language: automate, streamline, optimize, leverage, enhance, empower
- Each prompt includes 2-3 GOOD vs BAD example pairs
- Each prompt includes instruction: "Every candidate must reference a specific screen, UI element, or user action"
- All existing lens tests pass with updated prompts

## Files
- convex/analysis/lenses/extractTimeCompression.ts (prompt at lines 102-141)
- convex/analysis/lenses/extractEffortElimination.ts (prompt at lines 106-145)

title: M012-E004-S001: Pipeline and Output Orchestration
description:
Mission: M012 - Double Three-Layer Measurement Spec
Epic: E004 - Pipeline, CLI, and Storage Integration
Story: S001 - Pipeline and Output Orchestration

## What
Wire the new measurement spec generator through generateAllOutputs and the pipeline. Call generateEntityJsonSchemas to populate jsonSchemas after parsing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- OutputsResult.measurement_spec uses new MeasurementSpec type
- generateAllOutputs calls generateMeasurementSpec with correct inputs
- generateAllOutputs calls generateEntityJsonSchemas on parsed result to populate jsonSchemas
- Progress callbacks fire: outputs_measurement_spec started/completed/failed
- Error handling: failure doesn't block other outputs, results in null
- analysis/types.ts re-exports updated MeasurementSpec type
- generate-all-outputs.test.ts passes with new mock LLM response fixture
- pipeline.test.ts passes with updated assertions
- Mock LLM fixture in mock-llm.ts returns new-format measurement spec JSON

## Files to Modify

- packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/pipeline.ts
- packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/types.ts

- packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/__tests__/pipeline.test.ts

## Context
Key change: after generateMeasurementSpec returns, call generateEntityJsonSchemas(result) to populate jsonSchemas. Mock fixture needs complete new-format response with perspectives structure.

### Summary
Wire the updated measurement spec generator (from E002/E003) into the pipeline orchestration layer. The main code change is in `generateAllOutputs` — after calling `generateMeasurementSpec`, call `generateEntityJsonSchemas(result)` to populate `jsonSchemas` on the returned MeasurementSpec. Update `analysis/types.ts` re-exports for the new type shape. Update mock LLM fixtures to return new-format perspective-grouped responses, and update all test assertions accordingly.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pipeline.test.ts` assertion `result.outputs.measurement_spec` checks for new structure (perspectives, jsonSchemas)
- [ ] `npm test -- --run` in `packages/mcp-server` exits with code 0

title: M012-E001-S003: Redesign MeasurementSpec and Update Exports
description:
Mission: M012 - Double Three-Layer Measurement Spec
Epic: E001 - Core Types and Zod Schemas
Story: S003 - Redesign MeasurementSpec and Update Exports

## What
Replace the MeasurementSpec type with the new perspective-grouped structure, remove old types, update all exports.

New MeasurementSpec: { perspectives: { customer: { entities: CustomerEntity[] }, product: { entities: ProductEntity[] }, interaction: { entities: InteractionEntity[] } }, jsonSchemas: EntityJsonSchema[], confidence: number, sources: string[], warnings?: string[] }

## Acceptance Criteria
- MeasurementSpec has perspectives.customer, perspectives.product, perspectives.interaction
- MeasurementSpec has jsonSchemas: EntityJsonSchema[], confidence, sources, warnings?
- MeasurementSpec does NOT have: events[], total_events, coverage, userStateModel[]
- Old types removed: TrackingEvent, EventProperty, MapsTo, PerspectiveDistribution, EntityDefinition, EntityPropertyDef
- Old schemas removed: TrackingEventSchema, MapsToSchema, PerspectiveDistributionSchema
- Perspective type kept ('customer' | 'product' | 'interaction')
- UserState/UserStateCriterion kept (used by lifecycle states)
- core/src/index.ts exports new types, removes old type exports
- core/src/schema/index.ts exports new schemas, removes old schemas
- OutputGenerationResult.measurement_spec uses new type
- All core type tests and schema tests pass
- npm run build succeeds for @basesignal/core

## Files to Modify
- packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts
- packages/core/src/schema/outputs.ts
- packages/core/src/index.ts
- packages/core/src/schema/index.ts
- packages/core/src/types/__tests__/outputs.test.ts
- packages/core/src/schema/__tests__/outputs.test.ts

## Context

Build types first so E002 has a target to parse into.

- [ ] AC9: `packages/core/src/schema/index.ts` exports `ProductEntitySchema`, `ProductActivitySchema`, `CustomerEntitySchema`, `CustomerActivitySchema`, `InteractionEntitySchema`, `InteractionActivitySchema`, `EntityPropertySchema`, `EntityJsonSchemaSchema` and does NOT export `TrackingEventSchema`, `MapsToSchema`, `PerspectiveDistributionSchema`, `EntityPropertyDefSchema`, `EntityDefinitionSchema`
- [ ] AC10: `OutputGenerationResult.measurement_spec` in `packages/core/src/types/outputs.ts` still uses the `MeasurementSpec` type (type reference unchanged, but type shape is now perspective-grouped)

title: Recognize onboarding and getting-started content patterns
description:
## Context

**Mission M002:** Discover and model multi-level activation from product content
**Epic M002-E002:** Enhanced Data Sources for Activation Discovery

**Story M002-E002-S002:** Recognize onboarding and getting-started content patterns

**Dependencies:** None (can be done in parallel with S001 and S003)

## Specification

The URL classifier recognizes onboarding-related paths across all page types,
including /getting-started, /onboarding, /first-steps, /quick-start patterns.
These are prioritized in crawling as they contain behavioral descriptions.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [unit] classifyPageType returns 'onboarding' for /getting-started paths
2. [unit] classifyPageType returns 'onboarding' for /onboarding paths
3. [unit] classifyPageType returns 'onboarding' for /quick-start paths
4. [unit] classifyPageType returns 'onboarding' for /first-steps paths
5. [unit] Onboarding pages are added to MUST_CRAWL_TYPES priority tier
6. [integration] filterHighValuePages prioritizes onboarding content over 'other' pages

## Guidance

Add onboarding path patterns to classifyPageType:

if (path.match(/^\/(getting-started|onboarding|quick-start|first-steps)(\/|$)/)) return "onboarding";

Add to priority tiers:
- Add "onboarding" to MUST_CRAWL_TYPES (highest priority for activation discovery)

These paths contain behavioral language describing what users should DO,
which is exactly what we need for activation level inference.

### Reference Files
- convex/lib/urlUtils.ts

---

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** `docs/plans/2026-02-05-onboarding-url-patterns-impl.md`

### Overview
Add onboarding page type recognition to classifyPageType() with single regex pattern, then add to MUST_CRAWL_TYPES for crawl priority.

### Tasks
4 TDD tasks covering:
- Unit tests for onboarding classification (all 4 path patterns + negative tests)
- Implement classifyPageType onboarding pattern
- Add onboarding to MUST_CRAWL_TYPES + integration test
- Full test suite verification

### Testing
- [ ] Run project test suite to verify changes

---

## Implementation Hints
Create src/components/product-profile/MeasurementSpecSection.tsx. Group events by category. Summary bar with totals. Table per category with collapsible rows. Category colors: activation=indigo, value=emerald, retention=amber, expansion=purple.

### Product Perspective
- Measurement spec is the actionable output answering "what should I track?"
- Categories map to P&L framework layers — scannable summary-first layout

### Technical Perspective
- Collapsible cards instead of table rows to avoid Radix div-in-tbody HTML violation
- Mental model: "category is a section, event is an expandable card"
- Mirrors existing FieldMappingsSection pattern

### Simplification Review

- Removed fixed category ordering constant — categories emerge from data
- Single file, no unnecessary abstractions

### Final Design
Single MeasurementSpecSection component: empty state guard → summary bar (total events, confidence, category counts) → per-category sections with colored headers → collapsible event cards with flex-row triggers → expandable properties Tables inside. Uses existing Table, Badge, and Collapsible primitives.

### Next Steps

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:
## Implementation Plan

### Overview
Build `MeasurementSpecSection` — a single-file display component that renders a measurement spec with summary stats, events grouped by category as collapsible cards, and expandable property detail tables.

### Task 1: Create MeasurementSpecSection component
**File:** `src/components/product-profile/MeasurementSpecSection.tsx`

**Props:**
```typescript
interface Props {
  measurementSpec: MeasurementSpec | null | undefined
}
```

**Structure (top to bottom):**

1. **Empty state guard** — if `!measurementSpec`, render "No measurement spec generated yet" message in a muted paragraph.

2. **Summary bar** — a flex row with:
   - Total events count (from `spec.total_events`)
   - Confidence score as percentage (e.g., `0.85` → `85%`)
   - Per-category count badges with category colors (group events, count per category)

3. **Category sections** — group `spec.events` by `event.category` using an inline reduce. For each category:
   - Section header: capitalized category name + count Badge with category color
   - Category colors: `activation=indigo`, `value=emerald`, `retention=amber`, `expansion=purple`

4. **Event cards** — each event is a `Collapsible` (not a table row, to avoid Radix div-in-tbody issue):
   - **Trigger (collapsed):** flex row with:
     - ChevronRight/ChevronDown toggle icon
     - Event name in `font-mono`
     - Description (truncated)
     - Trigger condition
     - `maps_to` badge:
       - `value_moment` → emerald "Value Moment"
       - `activation_level` → indigo "Activation L{level}"
       - `both` → purple "Both"
   - **Content (expanded):** bordered `Table` showing properties:
     - Columns: Name, Type, Description
     - Uses `Table`, `TableHeader`, `TableBody`, `TableRow`, `TableHead`, `TableCell`

**Imports:**
- `Table`, `TableBody`, `TableCell`, `TableHead`, `TableHeader`, `TableRow` from `../ui/table`
- `Badge` from `../ui/badge`
- `Collapsible`, `CollapsibleContent`, `CollapsibleTrigger` from `../ui/collapsible`
- `ChevronRight`, `ChevronDown` from `lucide-react`
- `MeasurementSpec` type from `convex/analysis/outputs/types`
- `useState` from `react`

**Pattern reference:** Follows `FieldMappingsSection.tsx` pattern — uses `useState` for open/close state per event, Collapsible wrapping, Badge for counts.

### Task 2: Create test file
**File:** `src/components/product-profile/MeasurementSpecSection.test.tsx`

**Test fixture:** A `MeasurementSpec` object with:
- 2+ categories (e.g., activation with 2 events, value with 1 event)
- Events with properties for expand testing
- Various `maps_to` types

**5 tests (matching acceptance criteria):**

1. **"summary shows total events, confidence score, and category breakdown counts"**
   - Render with fixture
   - Assert total events text present
   - Assert confidence percentage present
   - Assert category count badges present

2. **"events grouped by category with header and count badge"**
   - Render with fixture (2 activation events, 1 value event)
   - Assert "activation" header with "2" badge
   - Assert "value" header with "1" badge

3. **"event card shows name, description, trigger condition, maps_to badge"**
   - Render with fixture
   - Assert event name visible (in mono text)
   - Assert description, trigger condition visible
   - Assert maps_to badge text (e.g., "Value Moment")

4. **"click event card expands to show properties table"**
   - Render with fixture
   - Properties table NOT visible initially
   - Click event trigger
   - Properties table visible with name/type/description columns

5. **"empty state renders when no measurement spec exists"**
   - Render with `measurementSpec={null}`
   - Assert "No measurement spec generated yet" text
   - Assert no summary bar or category sections

**Setup function pattern:**
```typescript
function setup(measurementSpec: MeasurementSpec | null = null) {
  const user = userEvent.setup()
  render(<MeasurementSpecSection measurementSpec={measurementSpec} />)
  return { user }
}
```

No router mock needed (component has no navigation). Uses `getByRole`, `getByText`, `queryByText` as primary queries per testing skill.

### Task 3: Verify
- Run `npm test -- --run src/components/product-profile/MeasurementSpecSection.test.tsx`
- All 5 tests must pass
- Run `npm run lint` to check for issues

### Dependencies
- This task depends on `basesignal-ohe` (ProductProfilePage) which provides the shell. However, this component is self-contained and can be built independently — it receives data via props, not from hooks.
- No new Convex queries needed; data comes from parent via props.

### Risks
- None significant. Component is display-only with well-defined types.

---

title: [M013-E001-S003] Browser Auto-Open and Slug Routing
description:
## Story: M013-E001-S003 — Browser Auto-Open and Slug Routing
**Mission:** M013 — View Command — Browser-Based Profile Viewing
**Epic:** M013-E001 — View Command & HTTP Server

## Outcome
When the view server starts, it automatically opens the user's default browser. If a slug argument was provided (`basesignal view linear-app`), it opens directly to that product's report page. Otherwise it opens the product list. GET /{slug} routes are wired up to return a placeholder report page (full rendering in E002). If the slug doesn't exist in ProductDirectory, a 404 page with a back-to-list link is returned. The browser open can be suppressed with --no-open.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Server auto-opens the default browser to http://localhost:<port>/ after starting
- When slug argument is provided, browser opens to http://localhost:<port>/<slug> instead
- The --no-open flag suppresses browser auto-open (useful for testing/scripting)
- GET /<existing-slug> returns 200 with an HTML page (placeholder body, full report in E002)
- GET /<nonexistent-slug> returns 404 with an HTML page containing 'Product not found' and a link back to /
- ProductDirectory.exists(slug) is used to check slug validity
- Browser open uses child_process to call platform-appropriate open command (no new npm dependency)
- Tests verify: slug route returns 200 for existing product, 404 for nonexistent slug, 404 page contains back link
- npm run build succeeds
- All existing tests pass (npm run test:run)

## Files to Modify
- packages/cli/src/commands/view.ts

## Implementation Hints
- Use child_process.exec for browser open: 'open' on macOS, 'xdg-open' on Linux, 'start' on Windows
- Commander auto-parses --no-X flags: .option('--no-open', 'Don\'t open browser automatically')
- Sanitize slug from URL: only allow [a-z0-9-] to prevent path traversal
- Use ProductDirectory.exists(slug) before attempting to load data
- Don't test actual browser open in tests — use --no-open or extract openBrowser as testable function

title: M006-E002-S002: Add value-moment-tier weighting to ICP input aggregation
description:
## Story: M006-E002-S002

Add weighted scoring to aggregateICPInputs: tier_1_moments * 5 + tier_2_moments * 2 + occurrence_count. Roles with few but high-tier moments outrank roles with many low-tier moments.

### Acceptance Criteria
- RoleAggregation includes tier_2_moments and tier_3_plus_moments
- Sorting uses weighted score: tier_1 * 5 + tier_2 * 2 + occurrence_count
- A role with 2 T1 moments outranks a role with 10 T3 moments
- Roles with only T3+ moments sort to the bottom
- buildICPPrompt includes tier breakdown per role
- Existing tests updated to reflect new sort order

### Key Files
- convex/analysis/outputs/aggregateICPInputs.ts
- convex/analysis/outputs/generateICPProfiles.ts

---

## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Show explicit three-tier breakdown (T1 / T2 / T3+ counts) in LLM prompt, not the weighted score number
- LLM infers importance from ordering (sorted by weighted score) and tier counts
- Keep prompt signal sharp — no mechanical weighting formula exposed
- Weighted formula intentionally allows some volume signal — roles with many T3 moments still have real presence

### Technical Perspective
- Compute weighted score inline in sort comparator, not as stored field on RoleAggregation
- Formula simple enough for sort time — avoid storing derived state
- Use tier_3_plus_moments (not tier_3_moments) since tier 3 is the catch-all bucket
- Update both RoleAggregation and RoleInput types in parallel — inline aggregateRoles() is acknowledged tech debt (TODO line 175) but needs tier fields for prompt display

### Simplification Review
- Removed weighted_score field from RoleAggregation — compute only in sort comparator
- Kept tier_2_moments and tier_3_plus_moments as stored fields (required by AC, needed for prompt)
- Keep weightedScore as inline arrow, not a named export
- No new abstractions needed
- Rejected lexicographic sort suggestion — AC explicitly specifies weighted formula
- Rejected type consolidation — out of scope, existing TODO tracks separately

### Final Design
Add tier_2_moments and tier_3_plus_moments to RoleAggregation. Inline weighted score in sort comparator. Update RoleInput with tier_2_count and tier_3_plus_count. Update inline aggregateRoles() for consistency. Update buildICPPrompt to compact format: "Role: N occurrences (X T1, Y T2, Z T3+)". Update all tests.

### Next Steps
- Implementation plan should cover: RoleAggregation interface changes, aggregation loop updates, sort comparator replacement, RoleInput interface updates, aggregateRoles helper updates, buildICPPrompt format changes, test updates for all four files

---
*Design: docs/plans/2026-02-12-tier-weighting-icp-aggregation-design.md*
*Updated via /brainstorm-auto*

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:
## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Roles with high-tier value moments must rank above roles with many low-tier moments to ensure ICP generation focuses on core daily users
- The weighted formula ensures quality (T1/T2) trumps quantity (T3 volume)

### Technical Perspective
- Single composite score `tier_1 * 5 + tier_2 * 2 + occurrence_count` as sole sort key — no alphabetical tiebreaker needed
- Update inline `aggregateRoles` in generateICPProfiles.ts immediately for consistency rather than waiting for TODO replacement
- Use `tier_3_plus_moments` naming since tier 3 is the catch-all bucket

### Simplification Review
- Reviewer suggested multi-key sort instead of weighted formula — rejected because weighted formula is an explicit acceptance criterion
- Kept scope minimal: 4 files, no new abstractions, inline weightedScore arrow function
- No unnecessary exports or configuration

### Final Design
Add tier_2/tier_3+ tracking to RoleAggregation and RoleInput. Replace two-key sort with weighted composite score. Update buildICPPrompt to show T1/T2/T3+ breakdown. Update both test files.

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover the exact edit sequence across the 4 files, with test updates interleaved.

title: Define judgment criteria for activation level accuracy
description:
## Context

**Mission M002:** Discover and model multi-level activation from product content
**Epic M002-E004:** Activation Level Validation & Refinement

**Story M002-E004-S002:** Define judgment criteria for activation level accuracy

**Dependencies:** None (can be done in parallel with S001).

## Specification

A rubric defines how to judge whether extracted activation levels are
accurate and useful. Criteria include logical progression, measurability
of criteria, alignment with known product aha-moments, and evidence quality.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [manual] Rubric defines 'accurate' vs 'mostly accurate' vs 'inaccurate' ratings
2. [manual] Rubric includes criterion: levels show logical progression (weak → strong)
3. [manual] Rubric includes criterion: criteria are measurable (action + count)
4. [manual] Rubric includes criterion: primary activation aligns with product's core value
5. [manual] Rubric includes criterion: evidence supports the inferred levels
6. [manual] Rubric is documented in docs/plans/ for reuse

---

## Implementation Plan

**Plan document:** `docs/plans/2026-02-05-activation-judgment-rubric-impl.md`

### Overview
Create a markdown rubric document with three-tier rating scale, four pass/fail evaluation criteria, a worked Miro example, and common failure modes.

### Tasks
6 tasks covering:
- Create document structure with header and rating scale
- Add four evaluation criteria (progression, measurability, core value, evidence)
- Add worked Miro example with full scoring
- Add common failure modes section
- Verify complete document
- Commit

### Testing
- [ ] Verify all six sections present in document
- [ ] Verify acceptance criteria mapping

title: M010-E002-S001: System prompt and prompt builder for lifecycle states
description:
## M010-E002-S001: System Prompt and Prompt Builder for Lifecycle States

### Acceptance Criteria
- LIFECYCLE_STATES_SYSTEM_PROMPT is an exported string constant
- System prompt instructs LLM to generate 7 states: new, activated, engaged, at_risk, dormant, churned, resurrected
- System prompt requires product-specific time windows calibrated to product type
- System prompt requires measurable entry_criteria and exit_triggers using event_name + condition
- System prompt requires activated state to align with activation map primary activation level
- buildLifecycleStatesPrompt accepts identity, value moments, activation levels, activation map
- Prompt includes product category/business model for calibration
- Prompt includes value moment names and tiers as engagement signal evidence

### Proposed Solution
Create `packages/mcp-server/src/analysis/outputs/lifecycle-states.ts` with two exports following activation-map.ts pattern:
1. LIFECYCLE_STATES_SYSTEM_PROMPT — static constant with role statement, 7-state task, complete JSON example (2-3 representative states), calibration/alignment/value-moments rules
2. buildLifecycleStatesPrompt(identity, valueMoments, activationLevels, activationMap) — assembles 4 markdown sections: Product Identity, Activation Levels, Activation Map Summary, Value Moments

Key decisions:
- Individual parameters (not wrapper object) — follows activation-map.ts pattern
- Use industry directly as product category proxy
- No lifecycle type imports needed — file only builds strings
- System prompt shows complete JSON output structure with 2-3 examples

### Design Doc
docs/plans/2026-02-22-lifecycle-states-prompt-builder-design.md

comments.author: Timo Dechau
comments.text:
## Auto-Brainstorming Complete

### Product Perspective
- Use principled calibration over archetype heuristics — provide activation map timeframes as data, let LLM derive natural cadence
- Scales to unusual products without maintaining archetype lookup tables
- LLM reasoning is auditable (visible why it picks specific time windows)

### Technical Perspective
- Individual parameters `(identity, valueMoments, activationLevels, activationMap)` — no wrapper type needed
- Follows activation-map.ts pattern (individual params + string template)
- System prompt is a static constant, prompt builder returns assembled string

### Simplification Review
- Removed calibration multiplier formulas (at_risk = 2-3x, dormant = buffer)
- Removed redundant alignment rules (activated ↔ activation map — implied by data)
- Simplified activation map input to primary_activation_level + transition timeframes as text
- System prompt shows one example instead of inline type definitions

### Final Design
Two exports: `LIFECYCLE_STATES_SYSTEM_PROMPT` (static string) and `buildLifecycleStatesPrompt(identity, valueMoments, activationLevels, activationMap)` (returns user prompt string with 4 sections: Product Identity, Activation Levels, Activation Map Summary, Value Moments).

### Next Steps
Implementation plan should cover: exact type imports, prompt text, section formatting, test fixtures.
